ref: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
text: Piligu ni, Naawuni daa namla zuɣusaa mini dunia.

ref: The earth was barren, with no form of life; it was under a roaring ocean covered with darkness. But the Spirit of God was moving over the water. The First Day
text: Dunia ŋɔ daa ka taɣinamsi, di daa kul bela vau, ka zibisim limsi bɔɣ’ zilinli, ka Naawuni shia daa be kom zuɣusaa ginda.

ref: God said, “I command light to shine!” And light started shining.
text: Ka Naawuni daa yɛli, “Neesim, neema!” Ka neesim dii neei.

ref: God looked at the light and saw that it was good. He separated light from darkness
text: Ka Naawuni daa nya ka neesim maa viɛla, ka o woligi neesim maa ka chɛ zibisim.

ref: and named the light “Day” and the darkness “Night.” Evening came, then morning—that was the first day. The Second Day
text: Ka Naawuni daa boli neesim maa wuntaŋ’ ni, ka boli zibisim maa mi yuŋ. Ka di daa niŋ zaawuni, di nyaaŋa ka biɛɣu neei; ka di niŋ dabisili din daŋ tooni.

ref: God said, “I command a dome to separate the water above it from the water below it.”
text: Ka Naawuni daa yɛli, “Foliŋga, niŋmi kom ŋɔ sunsuuni m-pirigi kom maa buyi.”

ref: and named it “Sky.” Evening came, then morning—that was the second day. The Third Day
text: Ka Naawuni daa boli foliŋga maa “Zuɣusaa.” Ka di daa niŋ zaawuni, di nyaaŋa ka biɛɣu neei; ka di niŋ dabisili din pah’ ayi.

ref: God said, “I command the water under the sky to come together in one place, so there will be dry ground.” And that's what happened.
text: Ka Naawuni daa lan yɛli, “Kom din be foliŋga tiŋli ŋɔ laɣimmi dɔni luɣ’ yini, ka chɛ ka tiŋgban’ kuŋ yi n-dɔni.” Ka di daa shiri niŋ lala.

ref: God named the dry ground “Land,” and he named the water “Ocean.” God looked at what he had done and saw that it was good.
text: Ka Naawuni daa boli tiŋgban’ kuŋ maa “Tiŋgbani,” ka boli kom din laɣim do luɣ’ yini maa mi “Teeku.” Ka Naawuni daa nya li ka di viɛla.

ref: God said, “I command the earth to produce all kinds of plants, including fruit trees and grain.” And that's what happened.
text: Ka Naawuni daa lan yɛli, “Binyɛra din bindi tiŋgban’ ni ka mali biɛlim mini tihi din wala mali kpila balibu kam zaa, bilimiya.” Ka di daa shiri niŋ lala.

ref: The earth produced all kinds of vegetation. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Ka binyɛra din bindi tiŋgban’ ni ka mali biɛlim mini tihi din wala mali kpila balibu kam zaa shiri bili. Ka Naawuni daa nya li ka di viɛla.

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the third day. The Fourth Day
text: Ka di daa niŋ zaawuni, di nyaaŋa ka biɛɣu neei; ka di niŋ dabisili din pah’ ata.

ref: God said, “I command lights to appear in the sky and to separate day from night and to show the time for seasons, special days, and years.
text: Ka Naawuni daa lan yɛli, “Neesim shɛŋa neemiya zuɣusaa sagbana ni n-woligi wuntaŋ’ ni ka chɛ yuŋ,” ka neesim maa wuhiri shihira ni saha ni dabisa ni yuma;

ref: I command them to shine on the earth.” And that's what happened.
text: ka lan nyɛ zuɣusaa neesim din ne dunia ni. Ka di daa shiri niŋ lala.

ref: God made two powerful lights, the brighter one to rule the day and the other to rule the night. He also made the stars.
text: Ka Naawuni daa lan nam neesim kara ayi n-chɛ ka din galisi pam la ne wuntaŋ’ ni, ka chɛ ka din pa di zuɣu mi ne yuŋ; ka daa nam saŋmarisi gba.

ref: Then God put these lights in the sky to shine on the earth,
text: Ka Naawuni daa zaŋ ba niŋ foliŋga maa zuɣusaa ni bɛ ne dunia ŋɔ ni,

ref: to rule day and night, and to separate light from darkness. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: ka su wuntaŋ’ ni mini yuŋ, ka woligi neesim ka chɛ zibisim. Ka Naawuni daa nya li ka di viɛla.

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the fourth day. The Fifth Day
text: Ka di daa niŋ zaawuni, di nyaaŋa ka biɛɣu neei; ka di niŋ dabisili din pah’ anahi.

ref: God said, “I command the ocean to be full of living creatures, and I command birds to fly above the earth.”
text: Ka Naawuni daa lan yɛli, “Chɛ ka binnɛma pam be kom ni, ka chɛ ka noonsi be zuɣusaa sagbana tiŋli yiɣiri gindi dunia ni.”

ref: So God made the giant sea monsters and all the living creatures that swim in the ocean. He also made every kind of bird. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Ka Naawuni daa lan nam binnɛma zaɣ’ kara niŋ teeku ni, ka nam binnɛma balibu kam bɛn be kom ni chana ni noonsi balibu kam. Ka Naawuni daa nya ba ka bɛ viɛla.

ref: Then he gave the living creatures his blessing—he told the ocean creatures to increase and live everywhere in the ocean and the birds to increase everywhere on earth.
text: Ka o daa niŋ alibarika niŋ bɛ ni ka yɛli ba, “Dɔɣimiya ka wɔɣi n-nab’ pali teeku mini dunia.”

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the fifth day. The Sixth Day
text: Ka di daa niŋ zaawuni, di nyaaŋa ka biɛɣu neei; ka di niŋ dabisili din pah’ anu.

ref: God said, “I command the earth to give life to all kinds of tame animals, wild animals, and reptiles.” And that's what happened.
text: Ka Naawuni daa lan yɛli, “Binnɛma balibu kam, bɛnya tiŋgbani zuɣu; niɣi mini binnɛma bɛn gbamdi tiŋa ni mɔɣu ni biŋkɔbiri balibu kam.” Ka di shiri niŋ lala.

ref: God made every one of them. Then he looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Ka Naawuni daa nam mɔɣu ni biŋkɔbiri balibu kam zaa, ka nam niɣi balibu kam zaa, ka nam binnɛma bɛn gbamdi tiŋa balibu kam zaa. Ka Naawuni daa nya ba ka bɛ viɛla.

ref: God said, “Now we will make humans, and they will be like us. We will let them rule the fish, the birds, and all other living creatures.”
text: Di saha ka Naawuni daa lan yɛli, “Chɛliya ka ti nam ninsala ŋ-ŋmahindi timaŋa, ka o be kaman ti ni be shɛm ŋɔ, ka chɛ ka bɛ su zahim mini noonsi ni niɣi ni dunia ŋɔ zaa ni binnɛma balibu kam bɛn gbamdi dunia ŋɔ ni fukumsi.”

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Naawuni daa nam ninsala ŋ-ŋmahindila omaŋa, ka o be kaman Naawuni ni be shɛm. O daa nam ba la doo mini paɣa.

ref: God gave them his blessing and said: Have a lot of children! Fill the earth with people and bring it under your control. Rule over the fish in the ocean, the birds in the sky, and every animal on the earth.
text: Ka Naawuni daa niŋ alibarika niŋ bɛ ni ka yɛli ba, “Dɔɣimiya n-nab’ pali dunia ŋɔ n-su di fukumsi; ka su teeku puuni zahim mini noonsi ni binnɛma balibu kam bɛn be dunia ŋɔ ni.”

ref: And I have given the green plants as food for everything else that breathes, including animals, both wild and tame, and birds. And so it was.
text: ka zaŋ bimbilli kam din maha n-ti mɔɣu ni biŋkɔbiri balibu kam mini binnɛma balibu kam bɛn mali nyɛvili zaŋ tabili noonsi balibu kam ni binnɛma balibu kam bɛn gbamdi tiŋgban’ ni ni bɛ dira.” Ka di daa shiri niŋ lala.

ref: God looked at what he had done. All of it was very good! Evening came, then morning—that was the sixth day.
text: Ka Naawuni daa nya o ni nam binshɛɣukam maa zaa, ka di viɛl’ pam. Ka di daa niŋ zaawuni, di nyaaŋa ka biɛɣu neei; ka di niŋ dabisili din pah’ ayɔbu.

ref: So the heavens and the earth and everything else were created. The Seventh Day
text: Lala ka Naawuni daa nam zuɣusaa mini dunia ŋɔ ni di binshɛɣukam zaa naai.

ref: That's how God created the heavens and the earth. The Garden of Eden When the Lord God made the heavens and the earth,
text: Naawuni ni daa nam zuɣusaa mini dunia ŋɔ binshɛɣukam zaa shɛm m-bala. Idɛn puu Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ Naawuni ŋɔ ni daa nam dunia ŋɔ mini zuɣusaa dahin’ shɛli,

ref: no grass or plants were growing anywhere. God had not yet sent any rain, and there was no one to work the land.
text: tutuɣu daa na kani, ka binshɛli mi daa na bi bili yi tiŋgban’ ni na, dama Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ Naawuni ŋɔ daa bi chɛ ka saa mira, ka so mi daa kani kɔra;

ref: But streams came up from the ground and watered the earth.
text: amaa kom daa puhiri yirila tiŋ’ na m-mahigiri tiŋgbani zaa.

ref: The Lord God took some soil from the ground and made a man. God breathed life into the man, and the man started breathing.
text: Di saha ka Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ Naawuni ŋɔ daa zaŋ tiŋgban’ ni taŋkpaɣu n-nam ninsala, ka vuhi nyɛvili vuhim niŋ o nyee ni; ka ninsal’ leei ŋun mali nyɛvili.

ref: The Lord made a garden in a place called Eden, which was in the east, and he put the man there.
text: Ka Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ Naawuni ŋɔ daa nam puu niŋ Idɛn din be wulimpuhili polo n-zaŋ o ni nam ninsal’ so maa niŋ nimaani.

ref: The Lord God filled the garden with all kinds of beautiful trees and fruit trees. Two other trees were in the middle of the garden. One of these gave life—the other gave the wisdom to know the difference between right and wrong.
text: Ka Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ Naawuni ŋɔ daa chɛ ka tia kam din lihibu viɛla ka simdi dibu bili yi tiŋgban’ ni na. Ka nyɛvili tia mini tia din chɛri ka nir’ baŋdi zaɣ’ suŋ ni zaɣ’ biɛɣu gba daa be puu maa sunsuuni.

ref: From Eden a river flowed out to water the garden, then it divided into four rivers.
text: Ka mɔɣili kom daa zɔri yiri Idɛn na n-teeni puu maa; ka di daa woligi n-niŋ mɔɣ’ anahi nimaani.

ref: The first one is the Pishon River that flows through the land of Havilah,
text: Mɔɣ’ shɛli din daŋ tooni yuli m-booni Pishɔn. Dina n-zɔri gindi Havila tiŋgbɔŋ zaa, ka salima be nimaani.

ref: where pure gold, rare perfumes, and precious stones are found.
text: Ka di tiŋgbɔŋ maa salima viɛl’ pam; ka tulaale tihi mini kuɣ’ viɛla gba be nimaani.

ref: The second is the Gihon River that winds through Ethiopia.
text: Ka mɔɣ’ shɛli din pah’ ayi yuli booni Gihɔn. Dina n-nyɛ din zɔri gindi Itiɔpia tiŋgbɔŋ.

ref: The Tigris River that flows east of Assyria is the third, and the fourth is the Euphrates River.
text: Ka mɔɣ’ shɛli din pah’ ata yuli booni Tigiris. Dina n-zɔri kpari Asiria wulimpuhili polo. Ka mɔɣ’ shɛli din pah’ anahi mi yuli booni Yufretiiz.

ref: The Lord God put the man in the Garden of Eden to take care of it and to look after it.
text: Ka Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ Naawuni ŋɔ daa zaŋ ninsal’ maa niŋ Idɛn puu maa ni ni o kɔr’ li, ka yuuni li.

ref: But the Lord told him, “You may eat fruit from any tree in the garden,
text: Ka Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ Naawuni ŋɔ daa yɛli ninsal’ maa, “Dim’ tihi din be puu ŋɔ ni wala;

ref: except the one that has the power to let you know the difference between right and wrong. If you eat any fruit from that tree, you will die before the day is over!”
text: amaa ti’ shɛli din chɛri ka nir’ baŋdi zaɣ’ suŋ mini zaɣ’ biɛɣu la, miri ka a di li, dama a yi ti di li dahin’ shɛli, a ni kpi.”

ref: The Lord God said, “It isn't good for the man to live alone. I will make a suitable partner for him.”
text: Dimbɔŋɔ nyaaŋa ka Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ Naawuni ŋɔ daa yɛli, “Di bi simdi ni doo be o ko, n ni nam sɔŋda ŋun simd’ o ti o.”

ref: So the Lord God made him fall into a deep sleep, and he took out one of the man's ribs. Then after closing the man's side,
text: Dinzuɣu ka Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ Naawuni ŋɔ daa chɛ ka gom gbaai doo maa pam, o ni daa gbihiri saha shɛli maa, ka o daa yih’ o sapir’ kɔb’ yini ka daai nimaani nimdi ŋari;

ref: the Lord made a woman out of the rib. The Lord God brought her to the man,
text: ka zaŋ o ni daa yihi sapir’ kɔb’ shɛli maa nam paɣa n-zaŋ o ti doo maa.

ref: and the man exclaimed, “Here is someone like me! She is part of my body, my own flesh and bones. She came from me, a man. So I will name her Woman!”
text: Di saha ka doo maa daa yɛli, “Mmaŋmaŋ’ kɔbili mini mmaŋmaŋ’ niŋgbuŋ m-bɔŋɔ. O yuli m-booni paɣa, dama doo niŋgbuŋ ni ka o yina.”

ref: That's why a man will leave his own father and mother. He marries a woman, and the two of them become like one person.
text: Dinzuɣu ka doo ŋmaligir’ o ba min’ o ma sani n-zaŋd’ omaŋ’ tabind’ o paɣa, ka bɛ niriba ayi maa be kaman ninvuɣ’ yino la.

ref: Although the man and his wife were both naked, they were not ashamed.
text: Doo maa min’ o paɣ’ maa daa bela bɛ zaɣim, amaa ka di bi mali ba vi.

ref: The snake was sneakier than any of the other wild animals that the Lord God had made. One day it came to the woman and asked, “Did God tell you not to eat fruit from any tree in the garden?”
text: Wahu daa nyɛla yɛmbimbiɛrilana n-gari Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ Naawuni ŋɔ ni daa nam mɔɣu ni biŋkɔb’ shɛb’ zaa. Ka wahu daa ti bɔhi paɣ’ maa, “Naawuni yɛliya ni yi miri ka yi di puu ŋɔ ni ti’ shɛli wala?”

ref: The woman answered, “God said we could eat fruit from any tree in the garden,
text: Ka paɣ’ maa yɛli wahu maa, “Ti ni tooi di ti’ shɛŋa din be puu ŋɔ ni wala;

ref: except the one in the middle. He told us not to eat fruit from that tree or even to touch it. If we do, we will die.”
text: amaa Naawuni yɛl’ ti, ‘Miriya ka yi di ti’ shɛli din be puu ŋɔ sunsuuni ŋɔ wala bee n-shihi li gba, yi yi niŋ lala, yi ni kpi.’ ”

ref: “No, you won't!” the snake replied.
text: Amaa ka wahu maa yɛli paɣ’ maa, “Yi ku kpi.

ref: “God understands what will happen on the day you eat fruit from that tree. You will see what you have done, and you will know the difference between right and wrong, just as God does.”
text: Dama Naawuni mi ni yi yi di li, yi nini ni neei, ka yi be kaman ŋun’ Naawuni, ka mi zaɣ’ suŋ ni zaɣ’ biɛɣu.”

ref: The woman stared at the fruit. It looked beautiful and tasty. She wanted the wisdom that it would give her, and she ate some of the fruit. Her husband was there with her, so she gave some to him, and he ate it too.
text: Dinzuɣu paɣ’ maa ni daa nya ka tia maa wala lihibu mali kɔre, ka viɛl’ ni dibu, ka lan nyɛ ti’ shɛli din ni ti nir’ baŋsim maa, ka o pɔhigi li di, ka zaŋ di shɛli ti ti o yidana, ka o gba di.

ref: At once they saw what they had done, and they realized they were naked. Then they sewed fig leaves together to cover themselves.
text: Di saha maa ka bɛ niriba ayi maa zaa nini daa shiri neei, ka bɛ nya ka bɛ bela bɛ zaɣim, ka bɛ daa ŋahi fiig tia vari she n-zaŋ so.

ref: Late in the afternoon, when the breeze began to blow, the man and woman heard the Lord God walking in the garden. So they hid behind some trees. Sin Brings a Curse
text: Ka Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ Naawuni ŋɔ daa ti be puu maa ni zaawun’ mahili saha n-chani ginda, ka doo maa min’ o paɣ’ maa wum o damli, ka zo nti sɔɣi puu maa ni tihi ni ni Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ Naawuni ŋɔ di nya ba.

ref: The Lord God called out to the man and asked, “Where are you?”
text: Amaa ka Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ Naawuni ŋɔ daa boli doo maa m-bɔh’ o, “Ya polo ka a lee be?”

ref: The man answered, “I was naked, and when I heard you walking through the garden, I was frightened and hid!”
text: Ka o garigi yɛli, “N wumla a damli puu ŋɔ ni ka dabiɛm gbaai ma, dama m bela n zaɣim, dina n-chɛ ka n sɔɣi maa.”

ref: “How did you know you were naked?” God asked. “Did you eat any fruit from that tree in the middle of the garden?”
text: Ka Naawuni bɔh’ o, “Ŋuni n-lee yɛl’ a ni a bela a zaɣim? A dila ti’ shɛli n ni yɛl’ a ni a miri ka a di la?”

ref: “It was the woman you put here with me,” the man said. “She gave me some of the fruit, and I ate it.”
text: Ka doo maa yɛli, “A ni zaŋ paɣ’ so ti ma ni o be n san’ la, ŋuna n-zaŋ tia maa walli maa ti ma ka n di.”

ref: The Lord God then asked the woman, “What have you done?” “The snake tricked me,” she answered, “and I ate some of that fruit.”
text: Ka Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ Naawuni ŋɔ bɔhi paɣ’ maa, “Bɔ n-niŋ ka a niŋ lala?” Ka paɣ’ maa yɛli, “Wahu n-yɔhim ma ka n di li.” Naawuni darigi alahichinim’ tibili

ref: So the Lord God said to the snake: “Because of what you have done, you will be the only animal to suffer this curse— For as long as you live, you will crawl on your stomach and eat dirt.
text: Ka Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ Naawuni ŋɔ daa yɛli wahu, “A ni niŋ shɛm ŋɔ zuɣu, n ni yɛli noli ti a n-gari yiŋ’ biŋkɔbiri mini mɔɣu ni biŋkɔbiri zaa; a ni nyɛritila a puli zuɣu, ka diri taŋkpaɣu hal ni a nyɛvil’ tariga.

ref: You and this woman will hate each other; your descendants and hers will always be enemies. One of hers will strike you on the head, and you will strike him on the heel.”
text: Ka n ni zaŋ dimdi niŋ a mini paɣ’ sunsuuni ni a zuliya min’ o zuliya sunsuuni, ka paɣ’ zuliya maa ni ti ŋme m-bɔr’ a zuɣu, ka a mi zuliya ti dim o zuliya napɔŋkpaa.”

ref: Then the Lord God said to the woman, “You will suffer terribly when you give birth. But you will still desire your husband, and he will rule over you.”
text: Ka Naawuni daa yɛli paɣ’ maa, “N ni chɛ ka a nya biɛrim pam bihi dɔɣibu ni; a shiri ni nya biɛrim bihi dɔɣibu ni; amaa di zaa yoli, a kɔre kul ni be a yidan’ ni, ka o ni su a.”

ref: The Lord said to the man, “You listened to your wife and ate the fruit I told you not to eat. And so, the ground will be under a curse because of what you did. As long as you live, you will have to struggle to grow enough food.
text: Ka Naawuni daa ŋmaligi yɛli Adam mi, “A ni deeg’ a paɣ’ noli, ka di ti’ shɛli n ni yɛl’ a ni a miri ka a di la zuɣu, n yɛli noli niŋ tiŋgban’ ni a zuɣu; a yɛn tumdila tuun’ kpɛma di ni n-nyari bindirigu diri hal ni a nyɛvil’ tariga.

ref: Your food will be plants, but the ground will produce thorns and thistles.
text: Ka gɔhi mini tutu’ shɛŋa din mali gɔhi ni bili tiŋgban’ ni a zuɣu; ka a ni diri tiŋgban’ ni tihi wala.

ref: You will sweat all your life to earn a living; you were made out of soil, and you will once again turn into soil.”
text: A ni tum tuun’ kpɛma ka woligu yiri a ni ka a nyari bindirigu diri hal ti lab’ tiŋgbani maa ni, dama di ni ka a daa yina. Taŋkpaɣu n-nyɛ a, ka a ni ti lan lab’ taŋkpaɣu maa ni yaha.”

ref: The man Adam named his wife Eve because she would become the mother of all who live.
text: Adam daa bol’ o paɣ’ maa yul’ la Awa, dama ŋuna n-daa nyɛ ninsalinim’ zaa ma.

ref: Then the Lord God made clothes out of animal skins for the man and his wife.
text: Ka Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ Naawuni ŋɔ daa zaŋ biŋkɔbiri gbana n-she n-ti Adam min’ o paɣ’ maa ka bɛ so. Naawuni yihi Adam mini Awa Idɛn puu ni

ref: The Lord said, “They now know the difference between right and wrong, just as we do. But they must not be allowed to eat fruit from the tree that lets them live forever.”
text: Ka Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ Naawuni ŋɔ daa yɛli, “Nyama, pumpɔŋɔ ninsal’ bemi kaman ti ni be shɛm, ka mi zaɣ’ suŋ ni zaɣ’ biɛɣu, ka di pa shɛli ka o ti pɔhigi nyɛvili tia la walli di m-be hal ni saha din ka bahigu.”

ref: So the Lord God sent them out of the Garden of Eden, where they would have to work the ground from which the man had been made.
text: Dinzuɣu ka Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ Naawuni ŋɔ daa kar’ o yihi Idɛn puu maa ni ni o ti kɔr’ o ni daa yi tiŋgban’ shɛli ni na maa.

ref: Then God put winged creatures at the entrance to the garden and a flaming, flashing sword to guard the way to the life-giving tree.
text: O ni daa kari ninsal’ yihi Idɛn puu maa ni maa, o daa zaŋla chɛrubinim’ mini takɔbi din nyɛ buɣim niɛm ka ŋmaligiri luɣilikam zali Idɛn puu maa wulimpuhili polo ni bɛ gu so’ shɛli din chani ti’ shɛli din tiri nyɛvil’ maa gbini.

ref: Adam and Eve had a son. Then Eve said, “I'll name him Cain because I got him with the help of the Lord.”
text: Ka doo maa min’ o paɣa Awa daa laɣim ka o niŋ pua n-dɔɣi Keen ka yɛli, “N nya ninsala Yawɛ sɔŋsim zuɣu.”

ref: Later she had another son and named him Abel. Abel became a sheep farmer, but Cain farmed the land.
text: Ka o daa ti lan dɔɣ’ o tizo Ɛbɛl gba. Ɛbɛl daa nyɛla piɛgula, ka Keen mi nyɛ pukpara.

ref: One day, Cain gave part of his harvest to the Lord,
text: Dahin shɛli ka Keen daa ti zaŋ o puu ni bindira na nti mali sara Yawɛ sani,

ref: and Abel also gave an offering to the Lord. He killed the first-born lamb from one of his sheep and gave the Lord the best parts of it. The Lord was pleased with Abel and his offering,
text: ka Ɛbɛl mi daa zaŋ o buzɔŋ ni piɛbil’ tuuli ni o kpam na nti mali sara, ka Yawɛ Naawuni nini daa tiɣi Ɛbɛl min’ o sara maa,

ref: but not with Cain and his offering. This made Cain so angry that he could not hide his feelings.
text: amaa ka o nini daa bi tiɣi Keen ŋun’ min’ o sara maa. Dinzuɣu ka Keen suhu daa yiɣisi pam, ka o zuŋ o ninni.

ref: The Lord said to Cain: What's wrong with you? Why do you look so angry?
text: Ka Yawɛ daa bɔhi Keen, “Bɔ n-niŋ ka a chɛ ka a suhu yiɣisi, ka a zuŋ a ninni?

ref: If you had done the right thing, you would be smiling. But you did the wrong thing, and now sin is waiting to attack you like a lion. Sin wants to destroy you, but don't let it!

text: Ka Ham ŋun daa nyɛ Keenan ba la daa nya o ba ni do o zaɣim maa, ka yi samban’ ni nti yɛl’ o mabih’ ayi la.

ref: Shem and Japheth put a robe over their shoulders and walked backwards into the tent. Without looking at their father, they placed it over his body.
text: Ka Shɛm mini Jafɛt daa kpuɣi kparibu m-pa bɛ bɔɣusapima zuɣu n-tirisi bɛ nyaaŋa kpe nti zaŋ li pa bɛ ba zuɣu, ka bi nya bɛ ba zaɣim.

ref: When Noah sobered up and learned what his youngest son had done,
text: Nuhu nini ni daa ti neei, ka bɛ yɛl’ o o bidibiga ŋun nyɛ biasani maa ni niŋ o shɛm.

ref: he said, “I now put a curse on Canaan! He will be the lowest slave of his brothers.
text: Ka o yɛli, “N yɛli noli ti Keenan. O ni nyɛla o biɛyanim’ dabili.”

ref: I ask the Lord my God to bless Shem and make Canaan his slave.
text: O daa lan yɛli, “Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ n Naawuni ŋɔ niŋm’ alibarika niŋ Shɛm ni; ka chɛ ka Keenan nyɛ o dabili.

ref: I pray God will give Japheth more and more land and let him take over the territory of Shem. May Canaan be his slave.”
text: Naawuni chɛ ka Jafɛt tɔŋ! Naawuni chɛ ka o zuliya mini Shɛm zuliya laɣim be; ka chɛ ka Keenan nyɛ o dabili.”

ref: and died at the age of
text: Nuhu daa niŋla yuun’ kɔbisiwɛi ni pihinu, ka naanyi yi dunia yili.

ref: After the flood Shem, Ham, and Japheth had many descendants. The Descendants of Japheth
text: Nuhu bidibisi, bɛn yuya daa booni Shɛm mini Ham ni Jafɛt la zuliya m-bɔŋɔ. Kom ni daa di dunia maa nyaaŋa, ka bɛ daa dɔɣi bidibisi.

ref: Japheth's descendants had their own languages, tribes, and land. They were Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras. Gomer was the ancestor of Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah. Javan was the ancestor of Elishah, Tarshish, Kittim, and Dodanim, who settled along the coast. The Descendants of Ham
text: Bɛmbɔŋɔ zuliya n-daa nyɛ ninvuɣ’ shɛb’ bɛn ʒini baɣili teeku. Bɛna n-nyɛ Jafɛt zuliya bɛn daa be bɛmaŋmaŋ’ tinsi ni, ka mali bɛmaŋmaŋ’ zuliyazuliya yɛtɔɣ’ kɔŋkɔba bɛ tiŋgban’ ni.

ref: This completes the list of Noah's descendants. After the flood their descendants became nations and spread all over the world.
text: Nuhu bidibisi maa zuliya mini bɛ dɔɣirikpamba yɛla m-bala. Bɛ ni ka zuliya kam yina n-nab’ pali dunia ŋɔ zaa, kom ni daa di dunia maa nyaaŋa.

ref: but after some of them moved from the east and settled in Babylonia,
text: Ka niriba daa ti yiɣisi wulimpuhili polo n-chaŋchaŋ ti paai Shiina tiŋgbɔŋ ni bɔpiɛligu ni n-kpalim ʒini nimaani.

ref: they said: Let's build a city with a tower that reaches to the sky! We'll use hard bricks and tar instead of stone and mortar. We'll become famous, and we won't be scattered all over the world.
text: Ka bɛ daa yɛl’ taba, “Kamiyana ka ti me timaŋmaŋ’ tiŋa, ka me pii tam di zuɣu ka di du hal ka di zuɣusaa ti tɔri sagbana n-zali yuli, dama di yi pa lala, ti ni ti wurim gili dunia ŋɔ zaa.”

ref: But when the Lord came down to look at the city and the tower,
text: Ka Yawɛ daa kana ni o ti nya ninsalinim’ ni me tin’ shɛli ka me pii tam di zuɣu maa.

ref: he said: These people are working together because they all speak the same language. This is just the beginning. Soon they will be able to do anything they want.
text: Ka Yawɛ daa yɛli, “Nyama, bɛ ni nyɛ zuliya yini ka mali zuliya yɛtɔɣ’ yini maa zuɣu ka bɛ tumdi lala maa, ka bɛ ni lo ni bɛ niŋ shɛm piligu n-na bɔŋɔ; ka binshɛli kani ka bɛ ni bɔri ni bɛ niŋ li ka di ku nani.

ref: Let's go down and confuse them! We'll make them speak different languages, and they won't be able to understand each other.
text: Chɛliya ka ti chaŋ bɛ sani nti chɛ ka bɛ yɛtɔɣa woli taba, ka di chɛ ka bɛ ku lan tooi wum tab’ yɛtɔɣa.”

ref: So the people had to stop building the city, because the Lord confused their language and scattered them all over the earth. That's how the city of Babel got its name. The Descendants of Shem
text: Ka bɛ daa boli nimaani yuli Babɛl, dama nimaani ka Yawɛ daa chɛ ka bɛ yɛtɔɣa woligiwoligi; nimaani ka o daa lan wurim ba kpɛhi dunia ŋɔ luɣ’ shɛlikam zaa. Shɛm daŋ niriba (

ref: Arpachshad had more children and died at the age of
text: Aripakishad ni daa dɔɣi Shɛla maa nyaaŋa, o daa niŋla yuun’ kɔbisinahinaata. O daa lan dɔɣi bidibisi mini bipuɣinsi pahi.

ref: Shelah had more children and died at the age of
text: Shɛla ni daa dɔɣi Ɛba maa nyaaŋa, o daa niŋla yuun’ kɔbisinahinaata. O daa lan dɔɣi bidibisi mini bipuɣinsi pahi.

ref: Peleg had more children and died at the age of
text: Pɛlɛg ni daa dɔɣi Riu maa nyaaŋa, o daa niŋla yuun’ kɔbisiyinaawɛi. O daa lan dɔɣi bidibisi mini bipuɣinsi pahi.

ref: Reu had more children and died at the age of
text: Riu ni daa dɔɣi Sɛrug maa nyaaŋa, o daa niŋla yuun’ kɔbisiyinaayopɔin. O daa lan dɔɣi bidibisi mini bipuɣinsi pahi.

ref: Serug had more children and died at the age of
text: Sɛrug ni daa dɔɣi Nahɔr maa nyaaŋa, o daa niŋla yuun’ kɔbisiyi. O daa lan dɔɣi bidibisi mini bipuɣinsi pahi.

ref: Terah decided to move from Ur to the land of Canaan. He took along Abram and Sarai and his grandson Lot, the son of Haran. But when they came to the city of Haran, they settled there instead.
text: Ka Tɛra daa zaŋ o bia Ibram min’ o yaaŋa Lɔt ni Ibram paɣa Zarai m-pah’ omaŋ’ zuɣu, ka bɛ yi Ur din be Kalidianim’ tiŋgbɔŋ ni n-kuni Keenan tiŋgbɔŋ ni. Ka bɛ daa mali ti paai Haran n-kpalim ʒini nimaani.

ref: The Lord said to Abram: Leave your country, your family, and your relatives and go to the land that I will show you.
text: Ka Yawɛ daa ti yɛli Ibram, “Yim’ a ya ŋɔ min’ a ba yiŋa ni a dɔɣiriba ŋɔ sani n-chaŋ tiŋgbɔn shɛl’ ni n ni yɛn wuh’ a.

ref: I will bless you and make your descendants into a great nation. You will become famous and be a blessing to others.
text: N ni chɛ ka a zuliya galisi pam. N ni niŋ alibarika niŋ a ni, ka chɛ ka a yuli du pam, ka chɛ ka ninvuɣ’ shɛb’ gba ti nya alibarika a zuɣu.

ref: I will bless those who bless you, but I will put a curse on anyone who puts a curse on you. Everyone on earth will be blessed because of you.
text: Ninvuɣ’ shɛb’ bɛn niŋ alibarika niŋ a ni, n gba ni niŋ alibarika niŋ bɛ ni. Ka ninvuɣ’ shɛb’ bɛn yɛli noli ti a, n gba ni yɛli noli ti ba. Ka dunia ŋɔ zuliya kam zaa ni ti nya alibarika a zuɣu.”

ref: Abram went as far as the sacred tree of Moreh in a place called Shechem. The Canaanites were still living in the land at that time,
text: ka Ibram daa yi nimaani n-chaŋ Shɛkɛm ti ʒini Mɔre oki tia gbini. Keenannima n-daa be di tiŋgbɔŋ maa ni di saha maa.

ref: but the Lord appeared to Abram and promised, “I will give this land to your family forever.” Abram then built an altar there for the Lord.
text: Ka Yawɛ daa ka Ibram sanna ti yɛl’ o, “N ni zaŋ tiŋgbɔŋ ŋɔ ti a zuliya.” Di saha ka Ibram daa me sara maligu bimbini nimaani zali Yawɛ ŋun daa ka o sani maa na.

ref: Abram traveled to the hill country east of Bethel and camped between Bethel and Ai, where he built another altar and worshiped the Lord.
text: Ka Ibram daa yiɣisi nimaani n-chaŋ ti ti o suɣili Bɛtɛl wulimpuhili polo zoli zuɣu ʒini; ka chɛ Bɛtɛl bahi wulinluhili polo, ka chɛ Ai mi bahi wulimpuhili polo. Ka o daa me sara maligu bimbini zali Yawɛ nimaani gba n-jɛmd’ o di gbini.

ref: Later, Abram started out toward the Southern Desert. Abram in Egypt
text: Ka Ibram daa lan gbaai chandi zaŋ kpa Nɛgɛb polo. Ibram chaŋ Ijipti

ref: The crops failed, and there was no food anywhere in Canaan. So Abram and his wife Sarai went to live in Egypt for a while. But just before they got there, Abram said, “Sarai, you are really beautiful!
text: O ni daa ti yɛn kpe Ijipti saha shɛli ka o yɛl’ o paɣ’ Zarai, “M mi ni a nyɛla paɣ’ viɛlli, ka a lihibu mali kɔre.

ref: When the Egyptians see you, they will murder me because I am your husband. But they won't kill you.
text: Ka Ijiptinim’ yi ti nya a, bɛ ni yɛli, ‘O paɣa m-bala’, ka ku ma ka chɛ a.

ref: Please save my life by saying you are my sister.”
text: Yɛlim’ ba ni a nyɛla n tizɔpaɣa, ka bɛ niŋ ma nirilim a zuɣu, ka tiligi n nyɛvili a zuɣu.”

ref: As soon as Abram and Sarai arrived in Egypt, the Egyptians noticed how beautiful she was.
text: Ka Ibram ni daa ti kpe Ijipti, Ijiptinim’ daa shiri nyaya ka paɣ’ maa nyɛla paɣ’ viɛlli.

ref: The king's officials told him about her, and she was taken to his house.
text: Firawuna kpambaliba ni daa nya o, bɛ daa paɣ’ o pam Firawuna sani. Ka bɛ daa zaŋ o tahi Firawuna yiŋa.

ref: The king was good to Abram because of Sarai, and Abram was given sheep, cattle, donkeys, slaves, and camels.
text: Ka Firawuna daa niŋ Ibram nirilim viɛnyɛliŋga Zarai zuɣu. O daa ti Ibram piɛri mini buhi ni niɣi ni bundibisi ni bunnyama ni daba bɛn nyɛ dɔbba mini paɣiba ni laakumnima.

ref: Because of Sarai, the Lord struck the king and everyone in his palace with terrible diseases.
text: Amaa ka Yawɛ daa chɛ ka alɔbo dɔriti gbaai Firawuna min’ o yiŋnima, Ibram paɣ’ Zarai maa zuɣu.

ref: Finally, the king sent for Abram and said to him, “What have you done to me? Why didn't you tell me Sarai was your wife?
text: Ka Firawuna daa boli Ibram na nti bɔh’ o, “Bɔ n-niŋ ka a niŋ ma lala? Bɔ n-niŋ ka a bi yɛli ma ni o nyɛla a paɣa?

ref: Why did you make me believe she was your sister? Now I've married her. Take her and go! She's your wife.”
text: Bɔ n-niŋ ka a yɛli ni o nyɛla a tizɔpaɣa, ka n zaŋ o niŋ m-paɣa? Dinzuɣu n-nyɛ a paɣa! Zaŋm’ o chaŋ.”

ref: So the king told his men to let Abram and Sarai take their possessions and leave.
text: Ka Firawuna daa bah’ o niriba noli, ka bɛ zaŋ Ibram min’ o paɣa ni o ni mali binshɛlikam zaa yihi soli.

ref: Abram and Sarai took everything they owned and went to the Southern Desert. Lot went with them.
text: Ka Ibram min’ o paɣa daa zaŋ bɛ ni su binshɛlikam zaa n-yi Ijipti chaŋ Nɛgɛb, ka Lɔt daa dol’ o chaŋ.

ref: Abram was very rich. He owned many cattle, sheep, and goats, and had a lot of silver and gold.
text: Ibram daa mali buni pam. O daa mali biŋkɔbiri mini anzinfa ni salima.

ref: Abram moved from place to place in the Southern Desert. And finally, he went north and set up his tents between Bethel and Ai,
text: Ka o daa yi Nɛgɛb n-chaŋ hal ti paai Bɛtɛl luɣ’ shɛli o ni daa tuui kpa o tanti suɣili zali piligu ni, ka di be Bɛtɛl mini Ai sunsuuni la.

ref: where he had earlier camped and built an altar. There he worshiped the Lord.
text: O ni daa tuui me sara maligu bimbini zali luɣ’ shɛl’ la; nimaani ka Ibram daa jɛm Yawɛ.

ref: Lot, who was traveling with him, also had sheep, goats, and cattle, as well as his own family and slaves.
text: Ka Lɔt ŋun daa doli Ibram maa gba daa mali biŋkɔbbaligu mini niɣi ni tantinima,

ref: Abram said to Lot, “We are close relatives. We shouldn't argue, and our men shouldn't be fighting one another.
text: Ka Ibram daa yɛli Lɔt, “Di chɛ ka naŋgbankpeeni kpe m min’ a sunsuuni; a mi di chɛ ka naŋgbankpeeni kpe a biŋkɔbiguliba mini m biŋkɔbiguliba sunsuuni, dama ti nyɛla dɔɣim.

ref: There is plenty of land for you to choose from. Let's separate. If you go north, I'll go south; if you go south, I'll go north.”
text: Pa palo n-kul do a tooni ŋɔ? Woligim’ ka chɛ ma. A yi zaŋ kpa nuzaa polo, n ni zaŋ kpa nudirigu polo; ka a yi zaŋ kpala nudirigu polo, m mi ni zaŋ kpa nuzaa polo.”

ref: This happened before the Lord had destroyed the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah. And when Lot looked around, he saw there was plenty of water in the Jordan Valley. All the way to Zoar the valley was as green as the garden of the Lord or the land of Egypt.
text: Ka Lɔt daa kpuɣ’ o zuɣu lihi Jɔɔdɛn Mɔɣili polo n-nya ka kom kul tɛli di vinvamli ni luɣ’ shɛlikam zaa, ka di be kaman Yawɛ tihi puu la bee m-be kaman Ijipti tiŋgbɔŋ ni luɣ’ shɛli din be Zɔa polo la. Dimbɔŋɔ nyaaŋa ka Yawɛ daa naanyi ti saɣim Sɔdom mini Gomɔra la.

ref: So Lot chose the whole Jordan Valley for himself, and as he started out toward the east, he and Abram separated.
text: Ka Lɔt daa pii Jɔɔdɛn Mɔɣili vinvamli polo maa n-daa zaŋ kpa wulimpuhili polo. Bɛ ni daa woligi shɛm m-bala.

ref: Abram stayed in the land of Canaan. But Lot settled near the cities of the valley and put up his tents not far from Sodom,
text: Ka Ibram daa be Keenan tiŋgbɔŋ ni, ka Lɔt mi be vinvamli maa ni fɔntinsi ni, ka daa ti yiɣisi n-zaŋ o tanti suɣili chaŋ ti kpa Sɔdom.

ref: where the people were evil and sinned terribly against the Lord. Abram Moves to Hebron
text: Di saha Sɔdomnim’ daa nyɛla ninvuɣ’ biɛri ni bɛn tumd’ alahichi pam Yawɛ sani. Ibram kuli Hiburɔn

ref: After Abram and Lot had gone their separate ways, the Lord said to Abram: Look around to the north, south, east, and west.
text: Ibram mini Lɔt ni daa woligi maa nyaaŋa, ka Yawɛ daa yɛli Ibram, “Kpuɣim’ a zuɣu lihi a ni ʒe luɣ’ shɛli ŋɔ nuzaa polo mini nudirigu polo ni wulimpuhili polo ni wulinluhili polo.

ref: I will give you and your family all the land you can see. It will be theirs forever!
text: A ni nya tiŋgbɔn shɛli maa zaa, n ni zaŋ li ti a zuliya ka bɛ su li sahakam.

ref: I will give you more descendants than there are specks of dust on the earth, and someday it will be easier to count those specks of dust than to count your descendants.
text: N ni chɛ ka a zuliya ti bemi kaman bihigu la, ka nir’ yi ni tooi kali bihigu, o ni ti tooi kal’ a zuliya gba.

ref: Now walk back and forth across the land, because I am going to give it to you.
text: Yiɣisim’ doli tiŋgbɔŋ maa wɔɣilim mini di yɛliŋ nya, n ni zaŋ li ti a.”

ref: Abram took down his tents and went to live near the sacred trees of Mamre at Hebron, where he built an altar in honor of the Lord.
text: Dinzuɣu ka Ibram daa vuɣ’ o tanti suɣili n-chaŋ ti kpa Mamri oki ti’ shɛŋa din be Hiburɔn la gbini; ka daa me sara maligu bimbini zali nimaani jɛm Yawɛ.

ref: About this time, King Amraphel of Babylonia, King Arioch of Ellasar, King Chedorlaomer of Elam, and King Tidal of Goiim
text: Shiina Naa Amirafɛl mini Ɛlasa Naa Ariok ni Ɛlam Naa Chɛdolaoma ni Goiim Naa Tidal

ref: attacked King Bera of Sodom, King Birsha of Gomorrah, King Shinab of Admah, King Shemeber of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela, a city also known as Zoar.
text: daa ti lo tɔbu bɛ ʒiɛman’ ni n-chaŋ ni bɛ ti tuhi Sɔdom Naa Bɛra mini Gomɔra Naa Birisha ni Adima Naa Shinab ni Zɛbɔiim Naa Shɛmɛba ni Bɛla bee Zɔa naa.

ref: King Chedorlaomer and his allies had ruled these last five kings for twelve years, but in the thirteenth year the kings rebelled and joined forces in Siddim Valley, which is now covered by the southern part of the Dead Sea.
text: Bɛ daa jɛm Chɛdolaoma la yuun’ pinaayi, amaa yuun’ shɛli din daa pahiri yuun’ pinaata la ni ka bɛ daa zaɣisi ni bɛ ku lan jɛm o.

ref: A year later King Chedorlaomer and his allies attacked and defeated the Rephaites in Ashteroth-Karnaim, the Zuzites in Ham, and the Emites in Shaveh-Kiriathaim.
text: Yuun’ shɛli din daa ti pahiri yuun’ pinaanahi ni, ka Chɛdolaoma mini na’ shɛb’ bɛn daa sɔŋd’ o daa chaŋ ti tuhi nyaŋ Rɛfaim din be Ashitɛrɔtikarinaim tiŋgbɔŋ ni mini Zuzim din be Ham tiŋgbɔŋ ni ni Ɛmim din be Shavɛkiriataim tiŋgbɔŋ ni

ref: They also defeated the Horites in the hill country of Edom, as far as El-Paran, near the desert.
text: ni Hɔrinim’ bɛn be Ɛdom zoya ni la n-doli ba hal ni Ɛliparan din be mɔɣu ni tintariga la shee.

ref: They went back to the city of Enmishpat, better known as Kadesh. Then they captured all the land that belonged to the Amalekites, and they defeated the Amorites who were living in Hazazon-Tamar.
text: Ka bɛ daa ŋmaligi lab’ Ɛnmishipat bɛ ni booni luɣ’ shɛli Kadɛshi la. Ka bɛ daa tuhi nyaŋ Amalɛk tiŋgbɔŋ ni nim’ mini Amɔrinim’ bɛn daa be Hazazɔntarima la zaa.

ref: At Siddim Valley, the armies of the kings of Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, Zeboiim, and Bela fought the armies of King Chedorlaomer of Elam, King Tidal of Goiim, King Amraphel of Babylonia, and King Arioch of Ellasar. The valley
text: ka bɛ mini Ɛlam Naa Chɛdolaoma ni Goiim Naa Tidal ni Shiina Naa Amirafɛl ni Ɛlasa Naa Ariok ti gbaai tab’ tuhibu. Nanima anahi mini nanima anu n-daa lu tab’ zuɣu maa.

ref: was full of tar pits, and when the troops from Sodom and Gomorrah started running away, some of them fell into the pits. Others escaped to the hill country.
text: Kootaali tandɔri n-daa kul yɛrigi Siddim vinvamli maa ni; ka Sɔdom mini Gomɔra nanim’ la daa ŋmaligi guuri ka bɛ ni shɛb’ faai lu kootaali tandɔri maa ni, ka bɛn kpalim mi zo nti du zoli.

ref: Their enemies took everything of value from Sodom and Gomorrah, including their food supplies.
text: Ka bɛ dimnim’ maa daa kana ti kpuɣi bɛn’ Sɔdomnim’ mini Gomɔranim’ maa nɛma mini bɛ binshɛɣukam zaa chaŋ.

ref: They also captured Abram's nephew Lot, who lived in Sodom. They took him and his possessions and then left.
text: Hal bɛ daa gbaai Lɔt ŋun daa nyɛ Ibram tizo bia la gba, ka zaŋ o nɛma pahi chaŋ.

ref: At this time Abram the Hebrew was living near the oaks that belonged to Mamre the Amorite. Mamre and his brothers Eshcol and Aner were Abram's friends. Someone who had escaped from the battle told Abram
text: Di saha ka bɛ ni yino ŋun daa zo n-tiligi daa kana ti yɛli Ibram ŋun daa nyɛ Hiburu nira ka be Mamri ŋun daa nyɛ Amɔri nir’ la oki tihi gbini. Mamri min’ o mabihi bɛn daa nyɛ Ɛshikol mini Anɛr la daa nyɛla Ibram zɔnima.

ref: that his nephew Lot had been taken away. Three hundred and eighteen of Abram's servants were fighting men, so he took them and followed the enemy as far north as the city of Dan.
text: Ibram ni daa wum ni bɛ tuhi gbaag’ o dɔɣim maa chaŋ maa, ka o daa zaŋ o tɔbbi’ shɛb’ bɛ ni daa dɔɣ’ o yiŋ’ puuni, ka bɛ nyɛ tɔbutuhiri’ biinsi la ninvuɣ’ kɔbisita ni pisaayika n-kari bɛ naba hal ti paai Dan.

ref: That night, Abram divided up his troops, attacked from all sides, and won a great victory. But some of the enemy escaped to the town of Hobah north of Damascus,
text: Ka Ibram daa pirigipirig’ o tɔbbihi maa n-chaŋ ti liri ba yuŋ ŋ-ŋme n-kari ba hal ti paai Hɔba din be Damaskus nuzaa polo la.

ref: and Abram went after them. He brought back his nephew Lot, together with Lot's possessions and the women and everyone else who had been captured. Abram Is Blessed by Melchizedek
text: Ka o daa va bɛ nɛma zaa, ka zaŋ o dɔɣim Lɔt min’ o nɛma gba ni ninvuɣ’ shɛba ni paɣiba m-pahi labina. Mɛlikizɛdɛk niŋ alibarika niŋ Ibram ni.

ref: Abram returned after he had defeated King Chedorlaomer and the other kings. Then the king of Sodom went to meet Abram in Shaveh Valley, which is also known as King's Valley.
text: Ibram ni daa nyaŋ Chɛdolaoma mini na’ shɛb’ bɛn be o sani ka labirina saha shɛli, ka Sɔdom naa daa chaŋ ti tuh’ o soli Shave vinvamli ni bɛ ni daa booni luɣ’ shɛli Naa Vinvamli la.

ref: King Melchizedek of Salem was a priest of God Most High. He brought out some bread and wine
text: Ka Salɛm Naa Mɛlikizɛdɛk ŋun daa lan nyɛ Zallakudura Naa maligumaana la daa zaŋ bɔrɔbɔro mini wain na nti ti Ibram;

ref: and said to Abram: “I bless you in the name of God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth.
text: ka suhi alibarika niŋ o ni ka yɛli, “Zallakudura Naa ŋun nam zuɣusaa mini dunia ŋɔ niŋm’ alibarika niŋ Ibram ni!

ref: All praise belongs to God Most High for helping you defeat your enemies.” Then Abram gave Melchizedek a tenth of everything.
text: Ti paɣiri Zallakudura Naa ŋun chɛ ka a nyaŋ a dimnim’ ŋɔ!” Ka Ibram daa zaŋ binshɛɣukam zaa pia puuni zaɣ’ yiniyini yihi Mɛlikizɛdɛk zaka.

ref: The king of Sodom said to Abram, “All I want are my people. You can keep everything else.”
text: Ka Sɔdom naa maa daa yɛli Ibram, “Zaŋm’ niriba ŋɔ ti ma, amaa ka mali nɛma ŋɔ zaa.”

ref: Abram answered: The Lord God Most High made the heavens and the earth. And I have promised him
text: Amaa ka Ibram daa yɛli, “M pun po Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ Zallakudura Naa ŋun nam zuɣusaa mini dunia ŋɔ

ref: that I won't keep anything of yours, not even a sandal strap or a piece of thread. Then you can never say that you are the one who made me rich.
text: ni n ku zaŋ a gali bee a namdili mia bee a ni su binshɛlikam, ka a ti yɛli, ‘Mani n-chɛ ka Ibram leei bundana.’

ref: Let my share be the food that my men have eaten. But Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre went with me, so give them their share of what we brought back.
text: N ku zaŋ binshɛli naɣila n nachimba ŋɔ mini ninvuɣ’ shɛb’ bɛn doli ma chaŋ la ni pun di shɛl’ la; amaa naɣ’ chɛ ka Anɛr mini Ɛshikol ni Mamri n-yihi bɛ tarili ni nyɛ shɛli di ni.”

ref: Later the Lord spoke to Abram in a vision, “Abram, don't be afraid! I will protect you and reward you greatly.”
text: Dimbɔŋɔnim’ nyaaŋa ka Yawɛ daa yɛli Ibram ʒii nyabu puuni, “Ibram, di chɛ ka dabiɛm mal’ a, n ni gbib’ a ka chɛ ka a laara ti galisi pam.”

ref: But Abram answered, “Lord All-Powerful, you have given me everything I could ask for, except children. And when I die, Eliezer of Damascus will get all I own.
text: Ka Ibram daa bɔhi, “Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ n Duuma ŋɔ, bɔ ka a yɛn ti ma? Nyama, n na ka bia, ka daɣila Ɛliɛza ŋun nyɛ Damaskus nir’ la nti yɛn di n fali?”

ref: You have not given me any children, and this servant of mine will inherit everything.”
text: Ibram daa lan yɛli, “Nyama, a ni bi ti ma bia maa zuɣu, bɛ ni dɔɣi dab’ so n yiŋ’ puuni la nti yɛn di n fali.”

ref: The Lord replied, “No, he won't! You will have a son of your own, and everything you have will be his.”
text: Amaa ka Yawɛ daa yɛl’ o, “Pa ŋuna nti yɛn di a fali; amaŋmaŋ’ bia nti yɛn di a fali.”

ref: Then the Lord took Abram outside and said, “Look at the sky and see if you can count the stars. That's how many descendants you will have.”
text: Ka Yawɛ daa zaŋ o yi samban’ ni ti yɛl’ o, “Kpuɣim’ a zuɣu lihi zuɣusaa n-kali saŋmarisi, a yi ni tooi kali ba.” Di saha ka o daa yɛl’ o, “Lala ka a zuliya ti yɛn be.”

ref: Abram believed the Lord, so the Lord was pleased with him and accepted him. The Lord Makes Another Promise to Abram
text: Ka Ibram daa niŋ Yawɛ yɛda, ka Yawɛ daa lih’ o niŋsim maa ka di nyɛla wuntia.

ref: The Lord said to Abram, “I brought you here from Ur in Chaldea, and I gave you this land.”
text: Di saha ka o daa lan yɛli Ibram, “Mani n-nyɛ Yawɛ ŋun daa yih’ a Ur din be Kalidianim’ tiŋgbɔŋ ni na ni n ti zaŋ tiŋgbɔŋ ŋɔ ti a ka a su li.”

ref: Abram asked, “Lord God, how can I know the land will be mine?”
text: Amaa ka Ibram bɔhi, “Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ n Duuma ŋɔ, wula ka n lee yɛn niŋ baŋ ni n ni ti su li?”

ref: Then the Lord told him, “Bring me a three-year-old cow, a three-year-old female goat, a three-year-old ram, a dove, and a young pigeon.”
text: Ka o yɛl’ o, “Bom’ yuma ata naɣilaa mini yuma ata bunyaŋ ni yuma ata piɛlaa ni ŋmanʒee ni yiŋ’ ŋmani ti ma.”

ref: Abram obeyed the Lord. Then he cut the animals in half and laid the two halves of each animal opposite each other on the ground. But he did not cut the doves and pigeons in half.
text: Ka Ibram daa bo biŋkɔbiri maa mini ŋmana maa zaa na nti kɔri ba m-pirigipirigi ba buyiyi n-zaŋ ba sɔŋ tuhi taba. Amaa o daa bi pirigi ŋmana la bɛn’ buyiyi.

ref: And when birds came down to eat the animals, Abram chased them away.
text: Ka juri daa ti kana ni bɛ ti di ba, ka Ibram kari ba.

ref: As the sun was setting, Abram fell into a deep sleep, and everything became dark and frightening.
text: Wuntaŋ’ ni daa ti miri lubu saha shɛli, ka gom daa gbaai Ibram pam ka o dɔni gbihi, ka zibisim titali din mali dabiɛm pam daa zibig’ o sani.

ref: Four generations later, your descendants will return here and take this land, because only then will the people who live here be so sinful that they deserve to be punished.
text: Zaŋ chaŋ ʒiɛmana din pahir’ anahi ni ka bɛ ni ti lab’ kpe na, dama Amɔrinim’ tuumbiɛri na bi galisi yaɣi.”

ref: Sometime after sunset, when it was very dark, a smoking cooking pot and a flaming fire passed between the two halves of each animal.
text: Wuntaŋ’ ni daa ti lu ka di zibiri saha shɛli, ka buɣim nyokpilli mini buɣim mokpalli daa kana ti kpe nimdi din ŋmahiŋmahi doya maa sunsuuni gari.

ref: At that time the Lord made an agreement with Abram and told him: I will give your descendants the land east of the Shihor River on the border of Egypt as far as the Euphrates River.
text: Dindali maa ka Yawɛ daa lo Ibram alikauli yɛli, “N ni zaŋ tiŋgbɔŋ ŋɔ ti a zuliya. Zaŋ Ijipti mɔɣili noli hal ti paai Yufretiiz Mɔɣ’ tital’ la noli

ref: They will possess the land of the Kenites, the Kenizzites, the Kadmonites,
text: zaŋ tabili Kɛnnim’ mini Kɛnizinim’ ni Kadimɔninim’

ref: the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Rephaites,
text: ni Hitinim’ ni Pɛrizinim’ ni Rɛfaimnim’

ref: the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Girgashites, and the Jebusites.
text: ni Amɔrinim’ ni Keenannim’ ni Gɛrigashinim’ ni Jɛbusinim’ tiŋgbana la zaa.”

ref: Abram's wife Sarai had not been able to have any children. But she owned a young Egyptian slave woman named Hagar,
text: Ibram paɣa Zarai daa bi dɔɣi bihi. Zarai daa mali Ijipti dab’ bipuɣiŋga ka o yuli booni Haga.

ref: and Sarai said to Abram, “The Lord has not given me any children. Sleep with my slave, and if she has a child, it will be mine.” Abram agreed,
text: Ka Zarai daa ti yɛli Ibram, “Nyama, Yawɛ bi chɛ ka n dɔɣi bihi, dinzuɣu zaŋmi n dab’ bipuɣiŋga ŋɔ dɔni, di pa shɛli o ni dɔɣi bihi ti ma.” Ka Ibram daa saɣi Zarai ni yɛli shɛm maa.

ref: and Sarai gave him Hagar to be his wife. This happened after Abram had lived in the land of Canaan for ten years.
text: Ibram ni daa chaŋ ti ʒini Keenan tiŋgbɔŋ ni la yuun’ pia nyaaŋa, ka Ibram paɣ’ Zarai daa zaŋ o dabili Haga ŋun daa nyɛ Ijipti bipuɣiŋga la n-ti o yidana Ibram ka o niŋ o paɣa.

ref: Later, when Hagar knew she was going to have a baby, she became proud and treated Sarai hatefully.
text: Ka Ibram mini Haga daa dɔni, ka o niŋ pua. Haga ni daa nya ka o mali puu maa, ka o lihi ʒiɛm Zarai ŋun daa su o maa.

ref: Then Sarai said to Abram, “It's all your fault! I gave you my slave woman, but she has been hateful to me ever since she found out she was pregnant. You have done me wrong, and you will have to answer to the Lord for this.”
text: Di saha ka Zarai daa yɛli Ibram, “N ʒiɛmbu ŋɔ taali ʒi a zuɣu. Mani n-zaŋ n dab’ bipuɣiŋga ŋɔ ti a, ka o ti nya ka o mali pua, ka lihi ʒiɛm ma. Yawɛ ni woligi ti m min’ a.”

ref: Abram said, “All right! She's your slave—do whatever you want with her.” Then Sarai began treating Hagar so harshly that she finally ran away.
text: Amaa ka Ibram daa yɛli Zarai “Nyama, o nyɛla a dabili, ka a su o fukumsi; niŋm’ o a ni yu shɛm.” Ka Zarai daa yihi Haga halibiɛɣu ka o zo.

ref: Hagar stopped to rest at a spring in the desert on the road to Shur. While she was there, the angel of the Lord came to her
text: Ka Yawɛ malaika daa yi o zuɣu mɔɣu ni kulibɔn shɛli din daa be Shur soli polo

ref: and asked, “Hagar, where have you come from, and where are you going?” She answered, “I'm running away from Sarai, my owner.”
text: m-bɔh’ o, “Zarai dabili Haga, ya ka a lee yina bee ya ka a lee chana?” Ka o yɛli, “N yila n lan’ Zarai sani zɔra.”

ref: The angel said, “Go back to Sarai and be her slave.
text: Ka Yawɛ malaika maa yɛl’ o, “Labim’ a lan’ maa sani nti zaŋ amaŋ’ ti o.”

ref: I will give you a son, who will be called Ishmael, because I have heard your cry for help. And someday I will give you so many descendants that no one will be able to count them all.
text: Yawɛ malaika maa daa lan yɛli Haga yaha, “Nyama, a mali pua, a mi ti yɛn dɔɣila bidibiga, ka bol’ o yuli Ishimail, dama Yawɛ wum a nandahima kuhigu maa.

ref: But your son will live far from his relatives; he will be like a wild donkey, fighting everyone, and everyone fighting him.”
text: A bia maa ni ti bemi kaman mɔɣu ni buŋ’ la. O ti ku bɔri so yɛla, ka so gba ti ku bɔr’ o yɛla; ka o min’ o dɔɣiriba zaa ti ku tooi laɣim be.”

ref: Hagar thought, “Have I really seen God and lived to tell about it?” So from then on she called him, “The God Who Sees Me.”
text: Ka Haga daa boli Yawɛ ŋun tɔɣis’ o yɛtɔɣa maa yuli, “Naawuni ŋun nyara;” dama o daa yɛlimi, “Ka daɣila n shiri nyala Naawuni ka na be n nyɛvil’ ni n ni nya o maa nyaaŋa?”

ref: That's why people call the well between Kadesh and Bered, “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.”
text: Dinzuɣu ka bɛ daa boli kɔbiliga maa yuli Bɛrilahairoi; ka di do Kadɛshi mini Bɛrɛd sunsuuni.

ref: I will keep my solemn promise to you and give you more descendants than can be counted.”
text: N ni chɛ ka m min’ a lo tab’ alikauli, ka n chɛ ka a zuliya nab’ pam.”

ref: Abram bowed with his face to the ground, and God said:
text: Ka Ibram daa teei dɔni n-zaŋ o nina kpa tiŋa, ka Naawuni yɛl’ o,

ref: I promise that you will be the father of many nations. So now I'm changing your name from Abram to Abraham.
text: A mi yuli ku lan booni Ibram, amaa a yuli ni boonila Ibrahimma; dama n zaŋ a leela zuliya pam yaba.

ref: I will give you a lot of descendants, and they will become great nations. Some of them will even be kings.
text: N ni chɛ ka a nam zuliya pam, ka chɛ ka a zuliya yɛligi, ka nanim’ ti yi a zuliya ni na.

ref: I will always keep the promise I have made to you and your descendants, because I am your God and their God.
text: “Ka n ni chɛ ka m min’ a ni a zuliya mali alikauli din ni ʒiɛya sahakam bɛ ʒiɛmana ni. Ka n ni nyɛ a min’ a zuliya maa Naawuni.

ref: I will give you and them the land in which you are now a foreigner. I will give the whole land of Canaan to your family forever, and I will be their God.
text: Ka n ni zaŋ Keenan tiŋgbɔŋ zaa din nyɛ yi ni be tiŋgban’ shɛli puuni n-nyɛ saamba ŋɔ n-ti a min’ a zuliya maa, ka yi su li sahakam, ka n nyɛ bɛ Naawuni.”

ref: Abraham, you and all future members of your family must promise to obey me.
text: Ka Naawuni daa lan yɛli Ibrahimma, “Di simdi ni a min’ a zuliya maa gbibi n daalikauli maa bɛ ʒiɛmana zaa ni.

ref: As the sign that you are keeping this promise, you must circumcise every man and boy in your family.
text: Gunimiya, ka di nyɛ m mini ya daalikauli maa shihirili.

ref: Any man who isn't circumcised hasn't kept his part of the promise and cannot be one of my people.
text: Ka doo kam ŋun bi guni, bɛn’ yihim’ o o daŋ’ ni, o ni bi gbibi n daalikauli maa zuɣu.”

ref: Abraham, from now on your wife's name will be Sarah instead of Sarai.
text: Ka Naawuni daa yɛli Ibrahimma, “Zaŋ chaŋ a paɣ’ Zarai mi polo, di lan bol’ o Zarai, amaa pumpɔŋɔ zaŋ chana bolim’ o la Zara.

ref: I will bless her, and you will have a son by her. She will become the mother of nations, and some of her descendants will even be kings.
text: N ni niŋ alibarika niŋ o ni; ka din pahi nyɛla, n ni chɛ ka o dɔɣi bidibiga ti a. N shiri ni niŋ alibarika niŋ o ni, ka chɛ ka o leei zuliya pam yabipaɣa; ka nanim’ ni ti yi o zuliya ni na.”

ref: Then he asked God, “Why not let Ishmael inherit what you have promised me?”
text: Ka Ibrahimma daa yɛli Naawuni, “N suhir’ a, dolimi Ishimail nyɛvili”

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Amaa ka Naawuni daa yɛl’ o, “Aai, a paɣ’ Zara ni dɔɣi bidibiga ti a, ka a bol’ o yuli Yisahaku. Ka n ni lo a min’ o zuliya alikauli shɛli din ni be sahakam.

ref: However, I have heard what you asked me to do for Ishmael, and so I will also bless him with many descendants. He will be the father of twelve princes, and I will make his family a great nation.
text: N wum a ni suhi ma suhigu shɛli zaŋ chaŋ Ishimail gba polo maa. N ni niŋ alibarika niŋ o ni, ka chɛ ka o zuliya nab’ pam. O ni ti nyɛla nanim’ pinaayi yaba, ka n chɛ ka o zuliya ti galisi pam.

ref: But your son Isaac will be born about this time next year, and the promise I am making to you and your family will be for him and his descendants forever.
text: Amaa n ni zaŋ n daalikauli n-lola Yisahaku so Zara ni yɛn dɔɣi ti a yuuni na ŋ-ŋɔ tasaha la.”

ref: God finished speaking to Abraham and then left.
text: Naawuni ni daa tɔɣisi Ibrahimma yɛtɔɣa ŋɔ naai ka o daa chaŋ ka chɛ o.

ref: On that same day Abraham obeyed God by circumcising Ishmael. Abraham was also circumcised, and so were all the other men and boys in his household, including his servants and slaves. He was
text: Ka dɔbba bɛn zaa daa be o yiŋ’ puuni, ka nyɛ bɛ ni dɔɣi bidib’ shɛb’ mini bɛ ni daa zaŋ bɛ liɣiri da shɛb’ tinzunnim’ sani zaa gba daa guni.

ref: One hot summer afternoon while Abraham was sitting by the entrance to his tent near the sacred trees of Mamre, the Lord appeared to him.
text: Ka Yawɛ daa ti zaŋ omaŋ’ wuhi Ibrahimma Mamri oki tihi la gbini, o ni daa ʒi o tanti suɣili dunol’ ni wuntaŋ’ zuɣusaa saha la.

ref: Abraham looked up and saw three men standing nearby. He quickly ran to meet them, bowed with his face to the ground,
text: Ka Ibrahimma daa lihi nya ka dɔbba ata ʒiɛya mir’ o. O ni daa nya ba maa, ka o yiɣis’ o tanti suɣili dunoli maa ni n-zo n-chaŋ bɛ sani nti damdi ba tiŋa

ref: and said, “Please come to my home where I can serve you.
text: ka yɛli, “N dana, a nini yi tiɣi ma, yin’ zanim’ mani yi dabili ŋɔ sani.

ref: I'll have some water brought, so you can wash your feet, then you can rest under a tree.
text: Chɛliya ka bɛ niŋ kom biɛla tin ya na ka yi paɣi yi naba, ka ʒini tia ŋɔ gbini vuhi,

ref: Let me get you some food to give you strength before you leave. I would be honored to serve you.” “Thank you very much,” they answered. “We accept your offer.”
text: ka n chɛ ka bɛ niŋ bindirigu tin ya na, yi ni ka mani yi dabili ŋɔ sani ŋɔ na zuɣu, di nyaaŋa ka yi naanyi gari.” Ka bɛ yɛl’ o, “Niŋm’ a ni yɛli shɛm maa.”

ref: Abraham went quickly to his tent and said to Sarah, “Hurry! Get a large sack of flour and make some bread.”
text: Ka Ibrahimma daa niŋ yomyom kpe o tanti suɣili ni Zara sani nti yɛl’ o, “Toom’ a zim viɛlli la lita pisinaayi ŋ-ŋɔri li mali maha yomyom.”

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Ka Ibrahimma daa zo n-chaŋ o biŋkɔbiri sani nti gbaai naɣilabil’ polli ŋun viɛla n-ti o tumtumda, ka o kɔrig’ o mal’ o yomyom.

ref: He then served his guests some yogurt and milk together with the meat. While they were eating, he stood near them under the tree,
text: Di saha ka Ibrahimma daa zaŋ waagasi mini bihim ni naɣilabil’ kpabili so bɛ ni daa kɔrigi duɣi maa nimdi na nti zali bɛ tooni, ka ʒe bɛ sani tia maa gbini ka bɛ dira.

ref: and they asked, “Where's your wife Sarah?” “She is right there in the tent,” Abraham answered.
text: Di saha ka bɛ daa bɔh’ o, “Ka a paɣ’ Zara be ya?” Ka o yɛli ba, “O be tanti suɣili ŋɔ ni.”

ref: Abraham and Sarah were very old, and Sarah was well past the age for having children.
text: Di saha maa Ibrahimma mini Zara zaa daa kurigiya pam hal ka Zara bi lan nyari sɔrilim gba

ref: So she laughed and said to herself, “Now that I am worn out and my husband is old, will I really know such happiness?”
text: Ka Zara daa la o suhu ni ka yɛli, “N kurigiya, ka n yidan’ gba kurigi, ka n kɔre ni lan be doo ni?”

ref: The Lord asked Abraham, “Why did Sarah laugh? Does she doubt that she can have a child in her old age?
text: Ka Yawɛ bɔhi Ibrahimma, “Bɔzuɣu ka Zara la ka yɛli, ‘N ni kurigi shɛm ŋɔ, n ni tooi lan dɔɣi bia?’

ref: I am the Lord! There is nothing too difficult for me. I'll come back next year at the time I promised, and Sarah will already have a son.”
text: Shɛli bi to Yawɛ sani. N shiri ni ti labina yuuni na ŋ-ŋɔ tasaha, ka Zara ni ti dɔɣi bidibiga.”

ref: Sarah was so frightened that she lied and said, “I didn't laugh.” “Yes, you did!” he answered. Abraham Prays for Sodom
text: Amaa ka Zara daa chihi yɛli, “M bi la,” dama dabiɛm n-daa gbaag’ o. Ka o yɛli, “Aai, a laya.” Ibrahimma suhi ni o fa Sɔdom bahi

ref: When the three men got ready to leave, they looked down toward Sodom, and Abraham walked part of the way with them.
text: Di saha ka niriba maa daa yiɣisi nimaani n-chani ka lihiri Sɔdom polo; ka Ibrahimma daa chani doli ba ni o zali ba soli.

ref: The Lord said to himself, “I should tell Abraham what I am going to do,
text: Ka Yawɛ daa yɛl’ o suhu ni, “Di simdi ni n zaŋ n ni lo ni n niŋ shɛli sɔɣi Ibrahimma?

ref: since his family will become a great and powerful nation that will be a blessing to all the other nations on earth.
text: Dama o zuliya ni ti galisi ka mali kpiɔŋ, ka dunia ŋɔ niriba zaa ni ti nya alibarika la o zuɣu.

ref: I have chosen him to teach his family to obey me forever and to do what is right and fair. If they do, I will give Abraham many descendants, just as I promised.”
text: Dama mani m-piig’ o ni o wuh’ o bihi min’ o yiŋnim’ zaa ka bɛ doli man’ Yawɛ soli, ka tumdi wuntia tuma ni din tuhi; ka di zuɣu chɛ ka man’ Yawɛ zaŋ n ni lo Ibrahimma binshɛli alikauli la ti o.”

ref: ?” “Because of them, I won't destroy the city,” was the Lord's answer.
text: Di saha ka Yawɛ daa yɛli, “Bɛ ni yɛri Sɔdomnim’ mini Gomɔranim’ daalahichi yɛla shɛm galisiya.

ref: Now I am going down to see for myself if those people really are that bad. If they aren't, I would like to know.”
text: N ni chaŋ nimaani ti nya bɛ shiri tumdi bɛ ni yɛri ni bɛ tumdi shɛm maa zaa bee bɛ bi tumdi li.”

ref: The men turned and started toward Sodom. But the Lord stayed with Abraham.
text: Ka niriba maa daa ŋmaligi chani kpari Sɔdom polo, amaa ka Ibrahimma na kul ʒe Yawɛ tooni.

ref: And Abraham asked him, “Lord, when you destroy the evil people, are you also going to destroy those who are good?
text: Ka Ibrahimma daa chaŋ o sani ti bɔh’ o, “A shiri yɛn laɣimla wuntizɔriba mini ninvuɣ’ biɛri ku?

ref: You surely wouldn't let them be killed when you destroy the evil ones. You are the judge of all the earth, and you do what is right.”
text: Bɔzuɣu ka a yɛn niŋ lala? Bɔzuɣu ka a yɛn laɣim wuntizɔriba mini ninvuɣ’ biɛri ku, ka wuntizɔriba maa pahi ninvuɣ’ biɛri maa zuɣu nya tibidarigibo. Di niŋ lala! Nyin’ ŋun nyɛ dunianim’ zaa sariakarita ŋɔ ku niŋ din tuhi?”

ref: Abraham answered, “I am nothing more than the dust of the earth. Please forgive me, Lord, for daring to speak to you like this.
text: Ka Ibrahimma daa lan yɛli, “Mani ŋun nyɛ taŋkpaɣu ni tampiligim ŋɔ niŋ suhukpeeni tɔɣisiri n Duuma yɛtɔɣa.

ref: Then Abraham said, “I don't have any right to ask you, Lord, but what would you do if you find only
text: Ka Ibrahimma lan yɛli, “N na kul bɔri ni n yɛli n Duuma shɛli pahi. Ka a yi ti nyala wuntizɔriba pisi nimaani?” Ka o garigi yɛli, “Wuntizɔriba pisi maa zuɣu, n ku saɣim tiŋ’ maa.”

ref: After speaking with Abraham, the Lord left, and Abraham went back home.
text: Ka Yawɛ daa tɔɣisi Ibrahimma yɛtɔɣa maa naai ka ŋmaligi, ka Ibrahimma gba daa ŋmaligi kuli.

ref: That evening, while Lot was sitting near the city gate, the two angels arrived in Sodom. When Lot saw them, he got up, bowed down low,
text: Ka malaikanima ayi maa daa mali ti paai Sɔdom zaawuni, ka Lɔt daa ʒi Sɔdom tiŋ’ puuni dunol’ ni. Lɔt ni daa nya ba, ka o yiɣisi chaŋ ti tuhi ba soli n-damdi zaŋ o zuɣu silim tiŋa ka yɛli,

ref: and said, “Gentlemen, I am your servant. Please come to my home. You can wash your feet, spend the night, and be on your way in the morning.” They told him, “No, we'll spend the night in the city square.”
text: “N duumanima, ŋmaligimiya kpe mani yi dabili ŋɔ yiŋ’ na nti paɣi yi naba, ka gbe kpe n-sa daŋ yibu chaŋ yi chandi shee.” Ka bɛ yɛl’ o, “Aai, ti ni gbe solɔɣu zuɣu.”

ref: But Lot kept insisting, until they finally agreed and went home with him. He quickly baked some bread, cooked a meal, and they ate.
text: Amaa ka Lɔt daa balim ba pam; ka bɛ daa ŋmaligi dol’ o kpe o yiŋa; ka o daa chɛ ka bɛ mali bɔrɔbɔro din ka dabindi, ka o zaŋ li niŋ ba saani ka bɛ di.

ref: Before Lot and his guests could go to bed, every man in Sodom, young and old, came and stood outside his house
text: Ka bɛ daa na bi kpe gom, ka Sɔdom tiŋ’ puuni dɔbba bɛn nyɛ ninkura mini bipola zaa daa laɣimna nti teei gili yili maa,

ref: and started shouting, “Where are your visitors? Send them out, so we can have sex with them!”
text: ka boli Lɔt m-bɔh’ o, “Saamba bɛn kpe a yil’ ŋɔ yuŋ ŋɔ la lee be ya? Zaŋm’ ba na ka ti goli ba.”

ref: Lot went outside and shut the door behind him.
text: Ka Lɔt daa vooi dunoli yo ka yi bɛ sani nti yɛli ba,

ref: Then he said, “Friends, please don't do such a terrible thing!
text: “M mabihi, n suhiri ya, di tumiya tuumbiɛɣu ŋɔ.

ref: I have two daughters who have never had sex. I'll bring them out, and you can do what you want with them. But don't harm these men. They are guests in my home.”
text: Nyamiya, m mali bipuɣins’ ayi bɛn na ʒi dɔbba, chɛliya ka n zaŋ ba tin ya na ka yi niŋ ba yi ni bɔri shɛm; ka di niŋ ya niriba ŋɔ shɛli, bɛ ni kpe n yili ŋɔ zuɣu.”

ref: “Don't get in our way,” the crowd answered. “You're a foreigner. What right do you have to order us around? We'll do worse things to you than we're going to do to them.” The crowd kept arguing with Lot. Finally, they rushed toward the door to break it down.
text: Amaa ka Sɔdomnim’ maa yɛl’ o, “Miligim’ ka ti ti palo. A nyɛla sana kpe, ka bɔri ni a wuhi ti ti ni yɛn niŋ shɛm? A yi bi baŋ ti ni niŋ a din bie n-gari ti ni yɛn niŋ ba shɛm.” Ka bɛ daa kul dihiri Lɔt na hal ti paai dunoli m-bɔri ni bɛ ŋme n-kab’ li.

ref: But the two angels in the house reached out and pulled Lot safely inside.
text: Amaa ka niriba maa daa teei bɛ nuhi tirina n-vooi Lɔt kpɛhi duu bɛ sani, ka yo dunoli.

ref: Then they struck blind everyone in the crowd, and none of them could even find the door.
text: Di saha ka bɛ daa chɛ ka niriba bɛn ʒe dunol’ ni n-nyɛ ninkura mini bipola maa zaa zom, ka chani bɔbindi bɔri dunoli shee. Lɔt yi Sɔdom

ref: The two angels said to Lot, “The Lord has heard many terrible things about the people of Sodom, and he has sent us here to destroy the city. Take your family and leave. Take every relative you have in the city, as well as the men your daughters are going to marry.”
text: dama ti yɛn saɣimla yaŋɔ. Bɛ ni yɛri di niriba tuumbiɛri yɛla shɛm galisiya pam Yawɛ sani; dinzuɣu ka o tim ti na ni ti ti saɣim li.”

ref: Lot went to the men who were engaged to his daughters and said, “Hurry up and get out of here! The Lord is going to destroy this city.” But they thought he was joking, and they laughed at him.
text: Ka Lɔt daa yi n-chaŋ ti yɛl’ o deennim’ bɛn daa yɛn kpuɣ’ o bipuɣinsi la, “Yiɣisimiya n-zo n-yi yaŋɔ, dama Yawɛ yɛn saɣim li mi.” Amaa ka o deennim’ maa daa zaŋ o ni yɛli shɛm maa niŋ asahi.

ref: Early the next morning the two angels tried to make Lot hurry and leave. They said, “Take your wife and your two daughters and get away from here as fast as you can! If you don't, every one of you will be killed when the Lord destroys the city.”
text: Ka biɛɣu daa ti neerina ka malaikanim’ maa kpam Lɔt jaande yɛra, “Yiɣisim’ zaŋ a paɣ’ min’ a bipuɣins’ ayi bɛn be a sani ŋɔ n-zo n-yi yaŋɔ, di yi pa lala, yaŋɔnim’ ni yɛn nya tibidarigibo shɛli maa ni tabili ya.”

ref: At first, Lot just stood there. But the Lord wanted to save him. So the angels took Lot, his wife, and his two daughters by the hand and led them out of the city.
text: Amaa ka Lɔt daa bi lɛbira, ka Yawɛ daa zo ba nambɔɣu n-chɛ ka malaikanim’ maa gbah’ o min’ o paɣ’ ni o bipuɣins’ ayi maa nuhi ni n-vo ba yihi tiŋ’ maa tariga ti zali.

ref: When they were outside, one of the angels said, “Run for your lives! Don't even look back. And don't stop in the valley. Run to the hills, where you'll be safe.”
text: Bɛ ni daa zaŋ ba na ti zali maa nyaaŋa, ka bɛ daa yɛli ba, “Zomiya tiligi! Yi mi miriya ka yi ŋmaligi lihi yi nyaaŋa bee n-zani vinvamli ŋɔ ni luɣ’ shɛli. Kul zomiya ti du zoya ŋɔ ha, di yi pa lala, buɣim ni din ya.”

ref: Lot answered, “You have done us a great favor, sir. You have saved our lives, but please don't make us go to the hills. That's too far away. The city will be destroyed before we can get there, and we will be killed when it happens.
text: m mali zuɣusuŋ yi sani, ka yi niŋ ma nirilim pam tiligi n nyɛvili, amaa n ku tooi zo nti du zoya ŋɔ, dama di pa shɛli ka yɛlibiɛ’ shɛli ti paai ma nimaani ka n kpi.

ref: There's a town near here. It's only a small place, but my family and I will be safe, if you let us go there.”
text: Nyama, timbil’ din ʒiɛya ŋɔ nyɛla din mirina ka nir’ ni zo nti kpe di ni. Chɛliya ka n zo nti kpe di ni tiligi. Yi nya di ni nyɛ timbil’ shɛm, ka m mi ni tooi zo nti kpe di ni tiligi.”

ref: “All right, go there,” he answered. “I won't destroy that town.
text: Ka bɛ yɛl’ o, “Ti ni niŋ a ni yɛli shɛm maa gba, ti ku saɣim tin’ shɛli a ni yɛri maa.

ref: Hurry! Run! I can't do anything until you are safely there.” The town was later called Zoar because Lot had said it was small. Sodom and Gomorrah Are Destroyed
text: Niŋm’ yomyom zo nti kpe di ni, dama ka pa ni a paala nimaani, ti ku tooi niŋ shɛli.” Dinzuɣu ka bɛ daa boli tiŋ’ maa yuli Zɔa. Sɔdom mini Gomɔra Saɣimbu

ref: On the way, Lot's wife looked back and was turned into a block of salt.
text: Amaa ka Lɔt paɣ’ ŋun daa be o nyaaŋa daa ti ŋmaligi lih’ o nyaaŋa, ka leei yalim kuɣili.

ref: When God destroyed the cities of the valley where Lot lived, he remembered his promise to Abraham and saved Lot from the terrible destruction. Moab and Ammon
text: Naawuni ni daa saɣindi vinvamli ni tinsi maa saha shɛli maa, ka o daa teei Ibrahimma yɛla, ka yihi Lɔt o ni daa saɣindi tin’ shɛŋa din nyɛ Lɔt ni daa ʒi luɣ’ shɛl’ la n-tilig’ o. Mɔabnim’ mini Ammɔnnim’ Piligu

ref: Lot was afraid to stay on in Zoar. So he took his two daughters and moved to a cave in the hill country.

ref: One day his older daughter said to her sister, “Our father is old, and there are no men anywhere for us to marry.

ref: That night they got their father drunk, and the older daughter got in bed with him, but he was too drunk even to know she was there.

ref: The next day the older daughter said to her sister, “I slept with my father last night. We'll get him drunk again tonight, so you can sleep with him, and we can each have a child.”

ref: That night they got their father drunk, and this time the younger sister slept with him. But once again he was too drunk even to know she was there.
text: Ka bɛ daa lan niŋ wain maa ti bɛ ba ka o nyu dindali maa yuŋ; ka biasani maa gba daa kpe ka o min’ o ba ti dɔni, ka o ba bi mi o ni dɔni o sani saha shɛli ni o ni yiɣisi saha shɛli.

ref: The younger daughter named her son Benammi, and he is the ancestor of the Ammonites.

ref: he told everyone that his wife Sarah was his sister. So King Abimelech of Gerar had Sarah brought to him.

ref: Her husband is a prophet. Let her go back to him, and his prayers will save you from death. But if you don't return her, you and all your people will die.
text: Dinzuɣu pumpɔŋɔ, labisim’ doo maa paɣa ti o; dama o nyɛla anabi, ka o suhi Naawuni ti a ka a be. Amaa a yi bi labis’ o ti o, nyin’ baŋmi ni biɛhiŋ kani a min’ a ni su ninvuɣ’ shɛb’ zaa ni kpi.”

ref: After Abimelech had given Abraham some sheep, cattle, and slaves, he sent Sarah back
text: Di saha ka Abimilɛk daa zaŋ piɛri mini naɣilahi ni dabidɔbba ni paɣiba ti Ibrahimma, ka zaŋ o paɣ’ Zara gba labisi ti o.

ref: The time came when Sarah no longer had to nurse Isaac, and on that day Abraham gave a big feast. Hagar and Ishmael Are Sent Away

ref: God blessed Ishmael, and as the boy grew older, he became an expert at hunting with his bow and arrows. He lived in the Paran Desert, and his mother chose an Egyptian woman for him to marry. A Peace Treaty

text: Ka Ibrahimma daa wolig’ o piɛsah’ ayopɔin zali bɛ ko.

ref: So they called the place Beersheba, because they made a treaty there.

ref: When the treaty was completed, Abimelech and his army commander Phicol went back to the land of the Philistines.

text: Ka Ibrahimma daa ʒini Filistianim’ tiŋgbɔŋ maa ni n-nyɛ sana n-yuui pam.

ref: Some years later God decided to test Abraham, so he spoke to him. Abraham answered, “Here I am, Lord.”
text: Dimbɔŋɔ nyaaŋa ka Naawuni daa ti yɛn zahim Ibrahimma ka bol’ o, “Ibrahimma!” Ka Ibrahimma garigi yɛli, “N-nyɛ ma.”
