text: Ka Naawuni yɛl’ o, “Zaŋm’ a bigaŋ’ Yisahaku so a ni yuri pam la n-chaŋ Mɔria tiŋgbɔŋ ni nti zaŋ o mali sara shɛli bɛ ni zaŋdi nyɔri buɣim la nimaani n ni ti yɛn wuh’ a zo’ shɛli zuɣu.”

ref: So Abraham got up early the next morning and chopped wood for the fire. He put a saddle on his donkey and set out with Isaac and two servants for the place where God had told him to go.
text: Ka Ibrahimma daa ti daŋ yiɣisibu asibaasi m-pa o buŋ’ nyaampara, ka zaŋ o nachimba ayi min’ o bia Yisahaku pah’ omaŋ’ zuɣu, ka bo dari pahi ni o ti mali sara shɛli bɛ ni zaŋdi nyɔri buɣim la n-daa yi n-chaŋ katiŋa Naawuni ni daa wuh’ o luɣ’ shɛli maa.

ref: Three days later Abraham looked off in the distance and saw the place.
text: Di daba ata dali ka Ibrahimma daa ti lihi nya o ni chani luɣ’ shɛli maa ka di be katiŋ’ ha.

ref: He told his servants, “Stay here with the donkey, while my son and I go over there to worship. We will come back.”
text: Di saha ka Ibrahimma daa yɛl’ o nachimba maa, “Ʒiɛnya buŋ’ ŋɔ sani, ka m mini bia ŋɔ chaŋ kpe ha ti jɛm Naawuni ka labina.”

ref: Abraham put the wood on Isaac's shoulder, but he carried the hot coals and the knife. As the two of them walked along,
text: Ka Ibrahimma daa deei da’ shɛŋa o ni daa bo ni o ti mali bɛ ni zaŋdi sara shɛli nyɔri buɣim la n-ʒil’ o bia Yisahaku; ka omaŋmaŋ’ mi kpuɣi buɣim mini sua n-gbib’ o nuu ni; ka bɛ niriba ayi maa daa doli tab’ chana.

ref: Isaac said, “Father, we have the coals and the wood, but where is the lamb for the sacrifice?” “My son,” Abraham answered, “God will provide the lamb.” The two of them walked on, and
text: Ka Ibrahimma yɛl’ o, “M bia, Naawuni maŋmaŋ’ ni ti ti piɛ’ so ti ni yɛn zaŋ mali sara shɛli bɛ ni zaŋdi nyɔri buɣim maa.” Ka bɛ niriba ayi maa daa kul dol’ tab’ chana.

ref: when they reached the place that God had told him about, Abraham built an altar and placed the wood on it. Next, he tied up his son and put him on the wood.
text: Bɛ ni daa mali ti paai luɣ’ shɛli Naawuni ni daa wuh’ o maa, ka Ibrahimma me sara maligu bimbini zali nimaani, ka zaŋ dari maa pa di zuɣu viɛnyɛliŋga. Di saha ka o daa lo o bia Yisahaku maa mihi n-zaŋ o pa bimbini maa zuɣu dari maa zuɣu.

ref: He then took the knife and got ready to kill his son.
text: Di nyaaŋa ka Ibrahimma daa teeg’ o nuu kpuɣi sua ni o kɔrig’ o bia maa.

ref: But the Lord's angel shouted from heaven, “Abraham! Abraham!” “Here I am!” he answered.
text: Amaa ka Yawɛ malaika daa be alizanda ni ka bol’ o, “Ibrahimma, Ibrahimma!” Ka o saɣi yɛli, “N-nyɛ ma.”

ref: “Don't hurt the boy or harm him in any way!” the angel said. “Now I know that you truly obey God, because you were willing to offer him your only son.”
text: Ka malaika maa yɛl’ o, “Miri ka a zaŋ a nuu shihi bia maa bee n-niŋ o shɛli; dama pumpɔŋɔ m baŋya ni a shiri zɔri Naawuni hal ka bi mɔŋ o a bigaŋ’ ŋɔ.”

ref: Abraham looked up and saw a ram caught by its horns in the bushes. So he took the ram and sacrificed it instead of his son.
text: Ka Ibrahimma daa kpuɣ’ o zuɣu ŋmaligi lihi n-nya piɛlaa ka o ʒiɛya, ka o yila shɛbi tiwula ni. Ka Ibrahimma daa chaŋ ti gbaai piɛlaa maa na nti zaŋ o mali sara shɛli bɛ ni zaŋdi nyɔri buɣim la, ka chɛ o bia maa.

ref: Abraham named that place “The Lord Will Provide.” And even now people say, “On the mountain of the Lord it will be provided.”
text: Ka Ibrahimma daa boli nimaani yuli, “Yawɛ ni tahi na.” Ka niriba na kul booni li lala hal ni zuŋɔ. “Yawɛ zol’ la zuɣu, ŋun’ Yawɛ ni tahi na.”

ref: The Lord's angel called out from heaven a second time:
text: Ka Yawɛ malaika daa be alizanda ni ka boli Ibrahimma pahi buyi

ref: You were willing to offer your only son to the Lord, and so he makes you this solemn promise,
text: n-yɛli, “Man’ Yawɛ yɛliya ni a ni bi mɔŋ ma a bigaŋ’ ŋɔ zuɣu, n ni ŋmaligi po mmaŋa

ref: “I will bless you and give you such a large family, that someday your descendants will be more numerous than the stars in the sky or the grains of sand along the seashore. They will defeat their enemies and take over the cities where their enemies live.
text: ni n ni niŋ alibarika niŋ a ni, ka chɛ ka a zuliya nabi m-be kaman saŋmarisi mini teeku noli bihigu la. Ka a zuliya maa ni ti deei bɛ dimnim’ tinsi su;

ref: You have obeyed me, and so you and your descendants will be a blessing to all nations on earth.”
text: ka dunia ŋɔ zuliya kam ni ti nya alibarika la bɛ zuɣu; a ni deei n noli maa zuɣu.”

ref: Abraham and Isaac went back to the servants who had come with him, and they returned to Abraham's home in Beersheba. The Children of Nahor
text: Ka Ibrahimma daa lab’ o nachimba maa sani ka o mini ba ti lab’ kuli Bɛrishiiba, ka Ibrahimma ti kpalim be nimaani. Nahɔr zuliya

ref: Nahor also had another wife. Her name was Reumah, and she had four sons: Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
text: Ka Nahɔr mam ŋun yuli daa booni Riuma la mi daa dɔɣi Tɛba mini Gaham ni Tahash ni Maaka.

ref: he went to the Hittites and said,
text: Ka Ibrahimma daa ti yiɣis’ o kpim’ maa sani n-chaŋ ti yɛli Hitinima,

ref: “I live as a foreigner in your land, and I don't own any property where I can bury my wife. Please let me buy a piece of land.”
text: “N nyɛla sana m-be yi sani, timiya ma luɣ’ shɛli ka n zaŋ li leei n kpiimba sɔɣibu shee n-zaŋ n kpim’ ŋɔ ti sɔɣi nimaani.”

ref: “Sir,” they answered, “you are an important man. Choose the best place to bury your wife. None of us would refuse you a resting place for your dead.”
text: “Ti dana, wummi ti ni yɛn yɛl’ a shɛm ŋɔ, A nyɛla zuɣulaan’ titali ti sani. Ti ni so ku mɔŋ’ a o siliga bee n-yɛl’ a ni a di sɔɣ’ a kpima. Dinzuɣu piim’ a ni bɔri ti silisi ŋɔ ni shɛli n-zaŋ a kpim’ maa sɔɣi di ni.”

ref: and replied, “If you are willing to let me bury my wife here, please ask Zohar's son Ephron
text: ka yɛli ba, “Yi yi ni saɣi ka n sɔɣi n kpim’ maa kpe, yin’ wummiya n ni yɛn yɛli ya shɛm ŋɔ. Balimmiya Zɔhar bia Ɛfurɔn,

ref: to sell me Machpelah Cave at the end of his field. I'll pay what it's worth, and all of you can be witnesses.”
text: ka o zaŋ Makipɛla siliga din be o palo la tariga la ti ma yi ninni; ka n yo o di liɣiri ka di leegi n dini, ka m mali n kpiimba sɔɣiri nimaani.”

ref: Ephron was sitting there near the city gate, when Abraham made this request, and he answered,
text: Ɛfurɔn maŋmaŋ’ daa ʒila o tab’ Hitinim’ maa sani; ka ŋun’ Ɛfurɔn daa yɛli Ibrahimma o tab’ Hitinim’ bɛn zaa daa ʒi tiŋ’ maa dunoli shee maa sani,

ref: “Sir, the whole field, including the cave, is yours. With my own people as witnesses, I freely give it to you as a burial place for your dead.”
text: “N dana, wummi n ni yɛn yɛl’ a shɛm ŋɔ, n zaŋ palo maa mini siliga din be di ni maa zaa ti a n niriba ŋɔ ninni ni a sɔɣ’ a kpim’ maa nimaani.”

ref: Once again, Abraham bowed down
text: Ka Ibrahimma daa lan damdi tiŋgbɔŋ maa nim’ tiŋa,

ref: and said to Ephron, “In front of these witnesses, I offer you the full price, so I can bury my wife. Please accept my offer.”
text: ka yɛli Ɛfurɔn niriba maa zaa ninni, “Amaa a yi di saɣi n naan yo a palo maa liɣiri. Saɣimi dee’ li, ka n zaŋ n kpim’ maa ti sɔɣi nimaani.”

ref: So Abraham buried his wife Sarah in Machpelah Cave that was in the field
text: Dimbɔŋɔ nyaaŋa ka Ibrahimma daa zaŋ o paɣ’ Zara nti sɔɣi sil’ shɛli din daa be Makipɛla palo din be Mamri wulimpuhili polo la ni Keenan tiŋgbɔŋ ni.

ref: Abraham was now a very old man. The Lord had made him rich, and he was successful in everything he did.
text: Ibrahimma daa kurigiya pam, ka Yawɛ daa niŋ alibarika niŋ o binshɛɣukam zaa ni.

ref: One day, Abraham called in his most trusted servant and said to him, “Solemnly promise me
text: Ka Ibrahimma daa ti yɛl’ o yiŋ’ nachin’ kpɛm’ ŋun su o binshɛɣukam zaa fukumsi, “Zaŋm’ a nuu niŋ n gbalipina lɔŋni,

ref: in the name of the Lord, who rules heaven and earth, that you won't choose a wife for my son Isaac from the people here in the land of Canaan.
text: ka n chɛ ka a po Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ Naawuni ŋun nam zuɣusaa mini tiŋgbani ŋɔ ni a ku kpuɣi n ni kana ti be Keenannim’ shɛb’ ni ŋɔ bipuɣiŋga n-ti m bia Yisahaku ka o niŋ o paɣa.

ref: Instead, go back to the land where I was born and find a wife for him from among my relatives.”
text: Amaa ni a ni chaŋla n ya n dɔɣiriba sani ti bo paɣ’ na ti ti m bia Yisahaku ka o niŋ o paɣa.”

ref: But the servant asked, “What if the young woman I choose refuses to leave home and come here with me? Should I send Isaac there to look for a wife?”
text: Ka o nachin’ kpɛm’ maa daa bɔh’ o, “Ka paɣ’ maa yi ti bi saɣi ni o doli ma kuli tiŋgbɔn shɛl’ ni a ni be ŋɔ na, man’ zaŋm’ a bia maa kulis’ a ya maa?”

ref: “No!” Abraham answered. “Don't ever do that, no matter what.
text: Ka Ibrahimma yɛl’ o, “Miri ka a zaŋ m bia maa kulisi nimaani.

ref: The Lord who rules heaven brought me here from the land where I was born and promised that he would give this land to my descendants forever. When you go back there, the Lord will send his angel ahead of you to help you find a wife for my son.
text: Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ Naawuni ŋun be alizanda ni ŋɔ n-daa yihi ma m ba yiŋa ni n dɔɣiri’ tiŋ’ ni na, ka po n-yɛli ma, ‘N ni ti zaŋ tiŋgbɔŋ ŋɔ ti a zuliya.’ O ni tim o malaika ka o daŋ a tooni, ka a chaŋ nimaani ti kpuɣi paɣ’ na nti ti m bia maa ka o niŋ o paɣa.

ref: If the woman refuses to come along, you don't have to keep this promise. But don't ever take my son back there.”
text: Amaa paɣ’ maa yi ti bi saɣi ni o dol’ a na, dindina a yi m pɔri ŋɔ taali ni; ka lee miri ka a zaŋ m bia maa kulisi nimaani.”

ref: So the servant gave Abraham his word that he would do everything he had been told to do.
text: Ka Ibrahimma nachin’ kpɛm’ maa daa zaŋ o nuu niŋ Ibrahimma gbalipina lɔŋni, ka po pɔri zaŋ chaŋ o ni yɛli shɛm maa polo.

ref: Soon after that, the servant loaded ten of Abraham's camels with valuable gifts. Then he set out for the city in northern Syria, where Abraham's brother Nahor lived.
text: Di saha ka nachin’ kpɛm’ maa daa zaŋ o dan’ Ibrahimma laakumnim’ pia, ka deei pin’ suma balibu kam o sani n-yiɣisi chaŋ Nahɔr ya din be Mɛsopoteemia tiŋgbɔŋ ni la.

ref: When he got there, he let the camels rest near the well outside the city. It was late afternoon, the time when the women came out for water.
text: Saha shɛli paɣiba ni siɣiri kuliga zaawuni ka o daa chɛ ka laakumnim’ maa gbani tiŋ’ tariga kɔbiliga gbini.

ref: The servant prayed: You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships. Please keep your promise to him and let me find a wife for Isaac today.
text: Ka o daa yɛli, “Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ n dan’ Ibrahimma Naawuni ŋɔ, n suhir’ a, tim ma nasara zuŋɔ dabisili ŋɔ, ka niŋ n dan’ Ibrahimma nirilim maŋli.

ref: The young women of the city will soon come to this well for water,
text: N ni ʒe kɔbiliga ŋɔ gbini, ka yaŋɔ paɣisara ti kana ni bɛ ti ŋɔŋ kom.

ref: and I'll ask one of them for a drink. If she gives me a drink and then offers to get some water for my camels, I'll know she is the one you have chosen and that you have kept your promise to my master.
text: N yi ti yɛli bɛ ni paɣisar’ so, ‘N suhir’ a, suum’ a koyuli ŋɔ zali ka n nyu,’ ka o saɣi ka yɛli, ‘Nyuma, ka chɛ ka n tooi shɛl’ na ti ti a laakumnim’ ŋɔ gba ka bɛ nyu;’ nyin’ chɛ ka ŋuna n-nyɛ a ni bɔri ni so niŋ a dabili Yisahaku paɣ’ maa. Dina n-ni chɛ ka m baŋ ni a niŋ n dan’ maa nirilim maŋli.”

ref: Abraham's servant ran to her and said, “Please let me have a drink of water.”
text: Di saha ka nachin’ kpɛm’ maa daa guui ti tuh’ o soli yɛl’ o, “N suhir’ a, tim ma a koyuli ŋɔ ni kom biɛla ka n nyu.”

ref: “I'll be glad to,” she answered. Then she quickly took the jar from her shoulder and held it while he drank.
text: Ka Ribɛka daa suug’ o koyuli maa na yomyom gbibi ka yɛl’ o, “N dana, nyuma.”

ref: After he had finished, she said, “Now I'll give your camels all the water they want.” She quickly poured out water for them, and she kept going back for more, until his camels had drunk all they wanted.
text: Ka o daa kpaai kom maa n-niŋ laakumnim’ maa kɔnyuribin’ ni yomyom, ka lan guui lab’ kɔbiliga maa ni ni o ti ŋɔŋ na yaha n-daa ŋɔŋ kom ti o laakumnim’ maa zaa.

ref: Abraham's servant did not say a word, but he watched everything Rebekah did, because he wanted to know for certain if this was the woman the Lord had chosen.
text: Ka nachin’ kpɛm’ maa daa kul maag’ o ni n-lihir’ o ni o nya Yawɛ chɛ ka o dila nasara o chandi maa ni maa bee.

ref: The servant had brought along an expensive gold ring and two large gold bracelets. When Rebekah had finished bringing the water, he gave her the ring for her nose and the bracelets for her arms.
text: Laakumnim’ maa ni daa nyu kom maa naai, ka nachin’ kpɛm’ maa zaŋ nyee ni baŋa din nyɛ salima ka di tibisim nyɛ binzahindigu pirigili mini bans’ ayi din nyɛ salima ka di tibisim nyɛ binzahindigu bupia ti o ni o pir’ o nuhi ni,

ref: Then he said, “Please tell me who your father is. Does he have room in his house for me and my men to spend the night?”
text: ka yɛl’ o, “Wuhim’ ma a ba ni nyɛ so. Duri be a ba yiŋa ka ti ni gbe nimaani?”

ref: She answered, “My father is Bethuel, the son of Nahor and Milcah.
text: Ka Ribɛka daa yɛl’ o, “N nyɛla Bɛtuɛl bipuɣiŋga. Bɛtuɛl mi daa nyɛla Nahɔr mini Milika bidibiga.”

ref: We have a place where you and your men can stay, and we also have enough straw and feed for your camels.”
text: O daa lan yɛli, “Ti mali taɣitaɣi mini mɔri, ka palo beni ka yi ni gbe.”

ref: Then the servant bowed his head and prayed,
text: Ka nachin’ kpɛm’ maa daa zaŋ o zuɣu silim tiŋa n-jɛm Yawɛ

ref: Rebekah ran straight home and told her family everything.
text: Ka paɣisarili maa daa guui chaŋ ti zaŋ yɛlli ŋɔ yɛl’ o ma yiŋnima.

ref: Her brother Laban heard her tell what the servant had said, and he saw the ring and the bracelets she was wearing. So Laban ran out to Abraham's servant, who was standing by his camels at the well.
text: Leeban ni daa nya nintua la mini bansi la, ka wum o tizɔpaɣa Ribɛka ni yɛli ni doo maa yɛl’ o shɛm zuɣu, ka o daa guui chaŋ doo maa sani nti nya ka o ʒiɛla laakumnim’ maa sani kɔbiliga maa gbini.

ref: Then Laban said, “The Lord has brought you safely here. Come home with me. There's no need for you to keep on standing outside. I have a room ready for you in our house, and there's also a place for your camels.”
text: Ka o daa yɛli nachin’ kpɛm’ maa, “Nyin’ ŋun nyɛ Yawɛ daalibarika nir’ ŋɔ, bɔ n-niŋ ka a kpalim ʒe sambani ŋɔ ni? Kpɛmina, dama m pun mali duu zal’ a, ka mali laakumnim’ ŋɔ gba biɛhigu shee zali.”

ref: Abraham's servant went home with Laban, where Laban's servants unloaded his camels and gave them straw and feed. Then they brought water into the house, so Abraham's servant and his men could wash their feet.
text: Ka nachin’ kpɛm’ maa daa chaŋ ti kpe yili maa ni, ka Leeban daa paai laakumnim’ maa ʒiya, ka niŋ taɣitaɣi mini mɔri ti o ni o ti laakumnim’ maa. Di nyaaŋa ka Leeban daa tooi kom na ti ti o mini bɛn daa be o sani ni bɛ paɣi bɛ naba.

ref: After that, they brought in food. But the servant said, “Before I eat, I must tell you why I have come.” “Go ahead and tell us,” Laban answered.
text: Ka bɛ daa ti zaŋ bindirigu na ti zali nachin’ kpɛm’ maa ni o di, ka o yɛli, “Di yi pa ni n yɛlila daliri din tahi ma na, n ku di bindirigu maa.” Ka Leeban yɛl’ o, “Yɛlima.”

ref: The servant explained: I am Abraham's servant.
text: Ka o yɛli, “N nyɛla Ibrahimma nachin’ kpɛma.

ref: The Lord has been good to my master and has made him very rich. He has given him many sheep, goats, cattle, camels, and donkeys, as well as a lot of silver and gold, and many slaves.
text: Ka Yawɛ niŋ alibarika niŋ n dan’ maa ni pam ka o leei bundana. O ti o piɛri mini buhi ni niɣi ni anzinfa mini salima ni daba bɛn nyɛ dɔbba mini paɣiba ni laakumnim’ ni bunsi.

ref: Sarah, my master's wife, didn't have any children until she was very old. Then she had a son, and my master has given him everything.
text: Ka n dan’ maa paɣ’ Zara dɔɣi bidibiga ti o, o ninkurilim ŋɔ ni; ka n dan’ maa zaŋ o binshɛɣukam zaa ti o bia maa.

ref: I solemnly promised my master that I would do what he said. And he told me, “Don't choose a wife for my son from the women in this land of Canaan.
text: Ka o daa chɛ ka m po pɔri ka o yɛli ma, ‘Miri ka a kpuɣi n ni kana ti be Keenannim’ shɛb’ tiŋgbɔŋ ni ŋɔ bipuɣiŋga ti m bia maa ka o niŋ o paɣa;

ref: Instead, go back to the land where I was born and find a wife for my son from among my relatives.”
text: amaa chamila n ya n dɔɣiriba sani ti bo paɣ’ na ti ti m bia maa ka o niŋ o paɣa.’

ref: I asked my master, “What if the young woman refuses to come with me?”
text: Ka n daa bɔhi n dan’ maa, ‘Ka paɣ’ yi ti bi saɣi n-doli ma na?’

ref: My master answered, “I have always obeyed the Lord, and he will send his angel to help you find my son a wife from among my own relatives.
text: Amaa ka o daa yɛli ma, ‘Yawɛ so n ni doli ŋɔ ni chɛ ka a min’ o malaika doli chaŋ, ka ti a nasara ka a kpuɣi paɣa n yaannim’ ya ni n dɔɣiriba sanna ti ti m bia maa.

ref: When I came to the well today, I silently prayed, “You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships, so please lead me to a wife for his son
text: “Ka n ni di paai kɔbiliga maa gbinna zuŋɔ, ka n yɛli, ‘Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ n dan’ Ibrahimma Naawuni ŋɔ, a yi ni ti ma nasara n chandi ŋɔ ni,

ref: while I am here at the well. When a young woman comes out to get water, I'll ask her to give me a drink.
text: n ni ʒe kɔbiliga ŋɔ gbini, ka paɣisar’ so yi ti kana ni o ti ŋɔŋ kom, ka n yɛl’ o, “N suhir’ a, niŋm’ a koyuli ŋɔ ni kom biɛla ti ma ka n nyu,”

ref: If she gives me a drink and offers to get some water for my camels, I'll know she is the one you have chosen.”
text: ka o yɛli ma, “Nyuma, ka chɛ ka n ŋɔŋ ti a laakumnim’ ŋɔ gba ka bɛ nyu,” nyin’ chɛ ka ŋuna n-nyɛ nyin’ Yawɛ ni bɔri ni so leei n-dan’ bia paɣa maa.’

ref: Even before I had finished praying, Rebekah came by with a water jar on her shoulder. When she had filled the jar, I asked her for a drink.
text: Yɛtɔɣa maa di na kul bela n suhu ni, ka Ribɛka paana, ka o koyuli tam o bɔɣusapiŋ zuɣu, ka o gari siɣi kɔbiliga maa ni ti ŋɔŋ kom na. Ka n yɛl’ o, ‘N suhir’ a, tim ma kom ka n nyu.’

ref: She quickly lowered the jar from her shoulder and said, “Have a drink. Then I'll get water for your camels.” So I drank, and after that she got some water for my camels.
text: Ka o dii suug’ o koyuli maa o bɔɣusapiŋ zuɣu na ti ma ka yɛli, ‘Nyuma, ka chɛ ka n ŋɔŋ shɛli ti a laakumnim’ ŋɔ gba ka bɛ nyu.’ Ka n di nyu ka chɛ ka o ŋɔŋ na ti ti n laakumnim’ maa gba ka bɛ nyu.

ref: I asked her who her father was, and she answered, “My father is Bethuel the son of Nahor and Milcah.” At once I put the ring in her nose and the bracelets on her arms.
text: Di saha ka m bɔh’ o, ‘Ŋun’ bia n-lee nyɛ a?’ Ka o yɛli ma, ‘N nyɛla Bɛtuɛl bipuɣiŋga. Bɛtuɛl mi daa nyɛla Nahɔr mini Milika bidibiga.’ Ka n zaŋ nintua m-pir’ o, ka zaŋ bansi pir’ o.

ref: Then I bowed my head and gave thanks to the God my master Abraham worships. The Lord had led me straight to my master's relatives, and I had found a wife for his son.
text: Di saha ka n di zaŋ n zuɣu silim tiŋa n-jɛm Yawɛ, ka paɣi ŋun’ Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ n dan’ Ibrahimma Naawuni ŋun wuhi yɛlimaŋli so’ shɛli n ni yɛn doli kpuɣi ŋuna n dan’ maa dɔɣim bipuɣiŋga nti ti o bia maa.

ref: Now please tell me if you are willing to do the right thing for my master. Will you treat him fairly, or do I have to look for another young woman?
text: Dinzuɣu yi yi ni niŋ n dan’ maa din tuhi ni din nyɛ yɛlimaŋli, yin’ yɛlim’ ma, ka yi yi ku niŋ o, yin’ lan yɛlim’ ma. Ka n zaŋ kpa n nudirigu polo bee n nuzaa polo.”

ref: Laban and Bethuel answered, “The Lord has done this. We have no choice in the matter.
text: Ka Leeban mini Bɛtuɛl daa yɛl’ o, “Yɛlli ŋɔ ni yila Yawɛ sani ŋɔ na, dindina ti ku tooi yɛl’ a din bie bee din viɛl’ zaŋ chaŋ di polo.

ref: Take Rebekah with you; she can marry your master's son, just as the Lord has said.”
text: Nyama, n-nyɛ Ribɛka, zaŋm’ o kuli nti chɛ ka o leeg’ a dan’ bia maa paɣa kaman Yawɛ ni yɛli shɛm maa.”

ref: Then he gave clothing, as well as silver and gold jewelry, to Rebekah. He also gave expensive gifts to her brother and her mother.
text: Di saha ka nachin’ kpɛm’ maa daa yihi anzinfa mini salima binyara ni chinchina ti Ribɛka; ka ti o tizɔdoo maa min’ o ma gba nachiinsi binshɛŋa din liɣiri galisi pam.

ref: Abraham's servant and the men with him ate and drank, then spent the night there. The next morning they got up, and the servant told Rebekah's mother and brother, “I would like to go back to my master now.”
text: Di nyaaŋa ka o mini ninvuɣ’ shɛb’ bɛn daa dol’ o la daa naanyi di ka nyu, ka gbe nimaani. Ka biɛɣu daa neei ka o yiɣisi ka yɛli ba, “N suhiri soli ni n labi n dan’ sani.”

ref: “Let Rebekah stay with us for a week or ten days,” they answered. “Then she may go.”
text: Ka Ribɛka tizɔdoo maa min’ o ma daa yɛli, “Chɛ ka paɣisarili maa na kpalim be ti sani daba ayi kaman biɛɣu pia pahi ka naanyi chaŋ.”

ref: But he said, “Don't make me stay any longer. The Lord has already helped me find a wife for my master's son. Now let us return.”
text: Amaa ka nachin’ kpɛm’ maa yɛli ba, “Yawɛ ni ti ma nasara shɛm ŋɔ zuɣu, di bi simdi ni yi chɛ ka n lan kpalim be kpe, timiya ma soli ka n labi n dan’ sani.”

ref: They answered, “Let's ask Rebekah what she wants to do.”
text: Ka bɛ yɛl’ o, “Chɛ ka ti boli paɣisarili maa na nti bɔh’ o nya.”

ref: They called her and asked, “Are you willing to leave with this man at once?” “Yes,” she answered.
text: Ka bɛ daa boli Ribɛka na nti bɔh’ o, “A ni doli doo ŋɔ chaŋ?” Ka o garigi yɛli, “N ni dol’ o chaŋ.”

ref: So Isaac told him, “Serve me the wild meat, and I can give you my blessing.” Jacob gave him some meat, and he ate it. He also gave him some wine, and he drank it.
text: Di saha ka o daa yɛl’ o, “Zaŋm’ li ti ma na, ka n ŋubi m bia mɔɣu ni nimdi, ka niŋ alibarika niŋ a ni.” Ka Yaakubu daa zaŋ li ti o ka o di, ka o zaŋ wain pah’ o ka o nyu.

ref: Then Isaac said, “Son, come over here and kiss me.”
text: Di saha ka o ba Yisahaku daa yɛl’ o, “M bia, mirim’ na ti mɔɣisi n kpariŋ.”

ref: While Jacob was kissing him, Isaac caught the smell of his clothes and said: “The smell of my son is like a field the Lord has blessed.
text: Ka o daa mir’ o ti mɔɣis’ o kpariŋ; ka Yisahaku wum o binyɛra ʒiɛɣu ka niŋ alibarika niŋ o ni ka yɛli, “Wummi m bia ʒiɛɣu, di ŋmanila Yawɛ ni niŋ alibarika niŋ mɔ’ shɛl’ ni ʒiɛɣu.

ref: God will bless you, my son, with dew from heaven and with fertile fields, rich with grain and grapes.
text: Naawuni tim’ a sakom din mal’ alibarika, ka chɛ ka a tiŋgbani vuhira. Naawuni tim’ a kawana mini wain pam.

ref: Nations will be your servants and bow down to you. You will rule over your brothers, and they will kneel at your feet. Anyone who curses you will be cursed; anyone who blesses you will be blessed.”
text: Naawuni chɛ ka niriba balibu kam jɛm a, ka chɛ ka zuliya kam damd’ a tiŋa. Naawuni chɛ ka a su a dɔɣiriba, ka chɛ ka a mabihi damd’ a tiŋa. Ninvuɣ’ shɛb’ bɛn tu a, Naawuni ŋun’ tum’ ba, ka ninvuɣ’ shɛb’ bɛn niŋ alibarika niŋ a ni, Naawuni ŋun’ niŋm’ alibarika niŋ bɛ ni.” Iso suh’ o ba Yisahaku alibarika

ref: Right after Isaac had given Jacob his blessing and Jacob had gone, Esau came back from hunting.
text: Yisahaku ni daa niŋ alibarika niŋ Yaakubu ni naai, ka Yaakubu ŋmalig’ o ba Yisahaku sani yi, di daa bi yuui ka o biɛli Iso yi o mɔɣu gɔbu maa paa’ na.

ref: He cooked the tasty food, brought it to his father, and said, “Father, please sit up and eat the meat I have brought you, so you can give me your blessing.”
text: Ka o gba daa duɣi o ni yuri bindir’ nyaɣisili shɛli n-zaŋ na ti ti o ba. O daa yɛlila o ba, “M ba, yiɣisim’ ŋub’ a bia mɔɣu ni nimdi ka niŋ alibarika niŋ n ni.”

ref: “Who are you?” Isaac asked. “I am Esau, your first-born son.”
text: Ka o ba Yisahaku bɔh’ o, “Ŋuni n-lee bala?” Ka o yɛl’ o, “Mani a bituuli Iso.”

ref: Isaac started trembling and said, “Then who brought me some wild meat right before you came in? I ate it and gave him a blessing that cannot be taken back.”
text: Di saha ka Yisahaku ni daa dii sɔhiri ka o bɔhi, “Ka ŋuni n-lee pun go mɔɣu ku nimdi n-zaŋ na ti ti ma ka n ŋub’ di zaa, ka pun niŋ alibarika niŋ o ni pɔi ka a naanyi kana ŋɔ? O mi shiri nya alibarika.”

ref: Isaac answered, “Your brother tricked me and stole your blessing.”
text: Amaa ka o yɛl’ o, “A tizo pun kana ti yɔhim ma deeg’ a daalibarika.”

ref: Esau replied, “My brother deserves the name Jacob, because he has already cheated me twice. The first time he cheated me out of my rights as the first-born son, and now he has cheated me out of my blessing.” Then Esau asked his father, “Don't you have any blessing left for me?”
text: Ka Iso yɛli, “Buyi ka o ŋari ma maa. O yuli Yaakubu maa shiri ŋman’ o. O daa fa n kpamdi, ka lan fa n daalibarika.” Di saha ka o daa bɔh’ o ba, “Ka daɣila a bi chɛ alibarika shɛli zali ma?”

ref: “My son,” Isaac answered, “I have made Jacob the ruler over you and your brothers, and all of you will be his servants. I have also promised him all the grain and grapes that he needs. There's nothing left that I can do for you.”
text: Ka Yisahaku daa garigi yɛli Iso, “M pun zaŋ o leeg’ a dana, ka zaŋ o mabihi zaa leeg’ o daba; ka zaŋ kawana mini wain ti o. M bia, ka bɔ ka n ni tooi lan niŋ ti a mi?”

ref: “Father,” Esau asked, “don't you have more than one blessing? You can surely give me a blessing too!” Then Esau started crying again.
text: Ka Iso bɔh’ o ba, “M ba, ka daɣila alibarika zaɣ’ yini kɔŋko ka a kul mali? M ba, niŋm’ alibarika niŋ n gba ni.” Ka Iso daa duh’ o yee kum pam.

ref: So his father said: “Your home will be far from that fertile land, where dew comes down from the heavens.
text: Di saha ka o ba Yisahaku daa yɛl’ o, “A tiŋgbani ku vuhira, ka maligim mi ku lur’ a tiŋgban’ ni.

ref: You will live by the power of your sword and be your brother's slave. But when you decide to be free, you will break loose.”
text: A takɔb’ zuɣu ka a yɛn dira, ka jɛmd’ a tizo maa. Amaa a yi zaɣis’ amaŋ’ zuɣu, a ni yi o sulinsi ni.”

ref: Esau hated his brother Jacob because he had stolen the blessing that was supposed to be his. So he said to himself, “Just as soon as my father dies, I'll kill Jacob.”
text: Ka Iso daa je Yaakubu yɛlli, o ba ni daa niŋ alibarika shɛli niŋ o ni la zuɣu. Ka Iso daa yɛli, “Di ku lan yuui ka ti ba kpi, di saha n ni ku n tizo Yaakubu.”

ref: When Rebekah found out what Esau planned to do, she sent for Jacob and told him, “Son, your brother Esau is just waiting for a chance to kill you.
text: Amaa ka so daa zaŋ Ribɛka bituuli Iso ni yɛli shɛm maa ti yɛli Ribɛka, ka o daa tim ti bol’ o bia Yaakubu ŋun nyɛ biasani maa na ti yɛl’ o, “A biɛli Iso ni zaŋ shɛli maag’ omaŋ’ suhu nyɛla, o lɔrila nia ni o ku a.

ref: Now listen carefully and do what I say. Go to the home of my brother Laban in Haran
text: Dinzuɣu, m bia, wummi n ni yɛn wuh’ a shɛm ŋɔ, yiɣisim’ zo n-kuli n tizɔdoo Leeban sani Haran

ref: and stay with him for a while. When Esau stops being angry
text: nti be nimaani hal ka a biɛli suhuyiɣisili maa ti chɛ,

ref: and forgets what you have done to him, I'll send for you to come home. Why should I lose both of my sons on the same day?”
text: ka o suhu ti maai ka o tam a ni niŋ o shɛm maa yɛla. Di nyaaŋa ka n naan yi tim na ka bɛ ti zaŋ a na. Bɔzuɣu ka n yɛn kɔŋ m bidibis’ ayi zaa dahin yini?” Yisahaku tim Yaakubu Leeban sani

ref: Rebekah later told Isaac, “Those Hittite wives of Esau are making my life miserable! If Jacob marries a Hittite woman, I'd be better off dead.”
text: Di saha ka Ribɛka daa yɛli Yisahaku, “Hiti paɣiba ŋɔ zuɣu chɛ ka n suhu bi doya. Yaakubu yi kpuɣi Hiti paɣa ka o be kaman bɛmbɔŋɔ, bebu bukaata lan yɛn nyɛ bɔ n sani?”

ref: Isaac called in Jacob, then gave him a blessing, and said: Don't marry any of those Canaanite women.
text: Di saha ka Yisahaku daa boli Yaakubu na ti niŋ alibarika niŋ o ni ka yɛl’ o, “Miri ka a kpuɣi Keenan paɣa.

ref: Go at once to your mother's father Bethuel in northern Syria and choose a wife from one of the daughters of Laban, your mother's brother.
text: Yiɣisim’ chaŋ Mɛsopoteemia a ma ba Bɛtuɛl yiŋa nti kpuɣi a ma tizɔdoo Leeban bipuɣinsi ni yino niŋ a paɣa.

ref: I pray that God All-Powerful will bless you with many descendants and let you become a great nation.
text: Naawuni Kpiɔŋlan’ Naa niŋm’ alibarika niŋ a ni ka ti a nama, ka chɛ ka a zuliya nabi; ka a leei zuliya pam yaba.

ref: May he bless you with the land he promised Abraham, so that you will take over this land where we now live as foreigners.
text: O zaŋm’ o ni daa niŋ alibarika shɛli niŋ Ibrahimma ni la niŋ a min’ a zuliya bɛn be a san’ ni, ka yi deei tiŋgbɔn shɛli Naawuni ni daa zaŋ ti Ibrahimma, ka yi be di ni ŋɔ su!”

ref: Isaac then sent Jacob to stay with Rebekah's brother Laban, the son of Bethuel the Aramean. Esau Marries the Daughter of Ishmael
text: Ka Yisahaku daa chɛ ka Yaakubu kuli Mɛsopoteemia Leeban ŋun daa nyɛ Bɛtuɛl Aram nir’ bia, ka nyɛ Yaakubu mini Iso ma Ribɛka tizɔdoo la sani. Iso lan kpuɣi paɣ’ so

ref: Esau found out that his father Isaac had blessed Jacob and had warned him not to marry any of the Canaanite women. He also learned that Jacob had been sent to find a wife in northern Syria
text: Iso ni daa nya ka Yisahaku niŋ alibarika niŋ Yaakubu ni ka chɛ ka o kuli Mɛsopoteemia ni o ti kpuɣi paɣ’ nimaani maa, ka lan baŋ ni o ni daa niŋ alibarika niŋ o ni maa o daa yɛl’ o mi, “Miri ka a kpuɣi Keenan paɣa.”

ref: and that he had obeyed his father and mother.
text: Ka o daa lan nya ka Yaakubu deeg’ o ba min’ o ma noli ka shiri kuli Mɛsopoteemia.

ref: Esau already had several wives, but he now realized how much his father hated the Canaanite women.
text: Dinzuɣu Iso ni daa nya ka o ba Yisahaku nini bi tiɣi Keenan paɣiba maa,

ref: So he married Ishmael's daughter Mahalath, who was the sister of Nebaioth and the granddaughter of Abraham. Jacob's Dream at Bethel
text: ka o daa chaŋ Ishimail zuliya ni ti kpuɣi Ibrahimma bia Ishimail bipuɣiŋga ŋun yuli daa booni Mahalat ka o tizɔdoo nyɛ Nɛbaiɔt la na ti pah’ o ni daa pun mali paɣ’ shɛb’ maa zuɣu. Yaakubu zahim zahindi Bɛtɛl

ref: Jacob left the town of Beersheba and started out for Haran.
text: Ka Yaakubu daa yi Bɛrishiiba n-zaŋ kpa Haran polo.

ref: At sunset he stopped for the night and went to sleep, resting his head on a large rock.
text: Ka o daa ti paai luɣ’ shɛli ka wuntaŋ’ lu ka o kpalim gbe nimaani dindali maa yuŋ. O daa piila kuɣ’ yini nimaani sɔŋ n-dɔni zaŋ o zuɣu kuɣ’ li gbihi.

ref: In a dream he saw a ladder that reached from earth to heaven, and God's angels were going up and down on it.
text: Ka o daa ti zahim zahindi nya ka darigu ʒe tiŋa ka di zuɣusaa chaŋ ti tɔri alizanda, ka o nya ka Naawuni malaikanim’ dol’ li duri ka siɣirina.

ref: The Lord was standing beside the ladder and said: I am the Lord God who was worshiped by Abraham and Isaac. I will give to you and your family the land on which you are now sleeping.
text: Ka Yawɛ daa zani baɣil’ o ka yɛli, “Mani n-nyɛ Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ a yab’ Ibrahimma mini Yisahaku Naawuni, n ni zaŋ a ni do tiŋgban’ shɛli ni ŋɔ ti a min’ a zuliya;

ref: Your descendants will spread over the earth in all directions and will become as numerous as the specks of dust. Your family will be a blessing to all people.
text: ka a zuliya ni ti bemi kaman bihigu la, ka yi ni yɛligi kpa wulinluhili polo mini wulimpuhili polo ni nuzaa polo ni nudirigu polo, ka dunia ŋɔ zuliya zaa ni ti nya alibarika la a min’ a zuliya maa zuɣu.

ref: Wherever you go, I will watch over you, then later I will bring you back to this land. I won't leave you—I will do all I have promised.
text: M bɔri ni a baŋ ni n ni be a sani, ka gbib’ a luɣ’ shɛlikam a ni yɛn chaŋ, ka ni ti zaŋ a labisi tiŋgbani ŋɔ ni na. Dama n kul ku chɛ a hal ti niŋ n ni lo a alikauli ni n ni niŋ a shɛm maa.”

ref: Jacob woke up suddenly and thought, “The Lord is in this place, and I didn't even know it.”
text: Di saha ka Yaakubu daa neei gom ni ka yɛli, “Yɛlimaŋli, Yawɛ bela kpe ka m bi mi!”

ref: Then Jacob became frightened and said, “What a frightening place! It must be the house of God and the gateway to heaven.”
text: Ka dabiɛm daa kpe o ka o yɛli, “Kpe nyɛla dabiɛm zɔbu shee! Kpe nyɛla Naawuni yiŋa. Alizanda dunoli m-bɔŋɔ.”

ref: When Jacob got up early the next morning, he took the rock that he had used for a pillow and stood it up as a place of worship. Then he poured olive oil on the rock to dedicate it to God,
text: Ka Yaakubu daa daŋ yiɣisibu asibaasi n-kpuɣi kuɣ’ shɛli o ni daa zaŋ kuɣ’ o zuɣu la sa nimaani, ka booi kpam bahi di zuɣu.

ref: and he named the place Bethel. Before that it had been named Luz.
text: O daa boli nimaani yul’ la Bɛtɛl. Amaa nimaani yuli daa pun boonila Luz.

ref: Jacob solemnly promised God, “If you go with me and watch over me as I travel, and if you give me food and clothes
text: Di saha ka Yaakubu daa po pɔri yɛli, “Naawuni yi ni be n sani, ka gbib’ ma n ni chani luɣ’ shɛli ŋɔ, ka ti ma bindirigu ka n di, ka ti ma situra ka n ye,

ref: and bring me safely home again, you will be my God.
text: ka n ti lan labi m ba yiŋ’ yaha ni suhudoo, dindina Yawɛ n-ni nyɛ n Naawuni.

ref: This rock will be your house, and I will give back to you a tenth of everything you give me.”
text: Ka n ni zaŋ kuɣili ŋɔ sa luɣ’ shɛli ŋɔ ni leei nyin’ Naawuni yiŋa, ka a yi ti ma binshɛlikam zaa, biɛhiŋ kani, n ni yihi di bini pia kam ni zaɣ’ yini ti a.”

ref: As Jacob continued on his way to the east,
text: Di saha ka Yaakubu daa yiɣisi lu soli gbaai chandi m-mali hal ti paai wulimpuhili polɔnim’ tiŋgbɔŋ ni.

ref: he looked out in a field and saw a well where shepherds took their sheep for water. Three flocks of sheep were lying around the well, which was covered with a large rock.
text: Ka o daa ti lihi nya kɔbiliga ka di do mɔɣu ni, ka piɛri tiɣisi do ʒii buta gil’ li, dama kɔbiliga maa ni kom ka bɛ daa ŋɔŋdi nyuhiri ba, ka kuɣ’ yɛlinli daa pa di noli zuɣu.

ref: Shepherds would roll the rock away when all their sheep had gathered there. Then after the sheep had been watered, the shepherds would roll the rock back over the mouth of the well.
text: Ka piɛri maa zaa daa yi ti laɣim nimaani na, piɛguliba maa paarila kuɣili maa kɔbiliga maa noli zuɣu sɔŋ ka ŋɔŋ kom ti piɛri maa, ka lan labisi kuɣili maa pa kɔbiliga maa noli zuɣu.

ref: Jacob asked the shepherds, “Where are you from?” “We're from Haran,” they answered.
text: Ka Yaakubu daa bɔhi piɛguliba maa, “M mabihi, ka tiŋkani ka yi lee yina?” Ka bɛ yɛl’ o, “Ti yila Haran na.”

ref: Then he asked, “Do you know Nahor's grandson Laban?” “Yes we do,” they replied.
text: Ka o bɔhi ba, “Yi mi Leeban Nahɔr bia la?” Ka bɛ yɛl’ o, “Ti mi o.”

ref: “How is he?” Jacob asked. “He's fine,” they answered. “And here comes his daughter Rachel with the sheep.”
text: Ka o bɔhi ba, “O be wula?” Ka bɛ yɛl’ o, “O mal’ alaafee;” ka lan yɛli, “O bipuɣiŋga Reechɛl n-taɣiri piɛri kanna ŋɔ.”

ref: Jacob told them, “Look, the sun is still high up in the sky, and it's too early to bring in the rest of the flocks. Water your sheep and take them back to the pasture.”
text: Ka Yaakubu yɛli, “Nyama, wuntaŋ’ ni n-na bɔŋɔ, biŋkɔbiri maa taɣ’ kuli yiŋ’ saha na bi paai, dinzuɣu ŋɔŋmiya kom ti piɛri maa ka bɛ nyu, ka yi zaŋ ba chaŋ ka bɛ ti ŋub’ mɔri.”

ref: But they replied, “We can't do that until they all get here, and the rock has been rolled away from the well.”
text: Amaa ka bɛ yɛl’ o, “Ti ku tooi niŋ lala ka pa ni piɛri maa zaa laɣimmina pɔi ka bɛ naanyi paai kuɣili maa kɔbiliga maa noli, ka ti ŋɔŋ kom maa ti ba.”

ref: While Jacob was still talking with the men, his cousin Rachel came up with her father's sheep.
text: Yaakubu mini ba ni daa na kul tɔɣisiri saha shɛli maa, ka Reechɛl taɣ’ o ba piɛri paa’ na; dama ŋuna n-daa guli ba.

ref: He then kissed Rachel and started crying because he was so happy.
text: Di nyaaŋa ka Yaakubu daa gbaai Reechɛl kpariŋ mɔɣisi ka kum suhupiɛlli kuhigu pam.

ref: He told her that he was the son of her aunt Rebekah, and she ran and told her father about him.
text: Ka Yaakubu daa yɛli Reechɛl ni o nyɛla o ba dɔɣim, ka nyɛ Ribɛka bia, Ka Reechɛl daa guui kuli ti yɛl’ o ba.

ref: As soon as Laban heard the news, he ran out to meet Jacob. He hugged and kissed him and brought him to his home, where Jacob told him everything that had happened.
text: Leeban ni daa wum o tizɔpaɣ’ bia Yaakubu yɛla maa, ka o guui ka o sanna nti bɔb’ gbaag’ o m-mɔɣis’ o kpariŋ, ka zaŋ o kul’ o yiŋa. Ka Yaakubu daa yɛli Leeban binshɛlikam zaa.

ref: Laban said, “You are my nephew, and you are like one of my own family.” Jacob Marries Leah and Rachel After Jacob had been there for a month,
text: Ka Leeban yɛl’ o, “Yɛlimaŋli, a shiri nyɛla n ʒim.” Ka Yaakubu daa be o sani goli yini. Yaakubu tum tuma ti Leeban Reechɛl mini Liya zuɣu

ref: Laban said to him, “You shouldn't have to work without pay, just because you are a relative of mine. What do you want me to give you?”
text: Di saha ka Leeban daa bɔhi Yaakubu, “A ni nyɛ n dɔɣira maa zuɣu, a yɛn tumla tuma ti ma yoli? Yɛlim’ ma a ni bɔri ni n yo a shɛm?”

ref: Laban had two daughters. Leah was older than Rachel, but her eyes didn't sparkle, while Rachel was beautiful and had a good figure.
text: Liya nina daa dii ka alaafee, amaa ka Reechɛl ŋun daa viɛli pam ka o ninni kul nyɛlisira.

ref: Since Jacob was in love with Rachel, he answered, “If you will let me marry Rachel, I'll work seven years for you.”
text: Ka Yaakubu suhu daa yu Reechɛl ka o yɛli, “N ni tum tuma yuma ayopɔin ti a, Reechɛl ŋun nyɛ a bipuɣiŋga biasani ŋɔ zuɣu.”

ref: Laban replied, “It's better for me to let you marry Rachel than for someone else to have her. So stay and work for me.”
text: Ka Leeban yɛl’ o, “N yi zaŋ o ti a, di so ni n zaŋ o ti do’ so, kpalimmi n sani.”

ref: Jacob worked seven years for Laban, but the time seemed like only a few days, because he loved Rachel so much.
text: Dinzuɣu ka Yaakubu daa tum tuma yuma ayopɔin Reechɛl zuɣu, amaa ka di daa ŋmanila dabisa biɛla o sani, o ni daa yuri Reechɛl maa zuɣu.

ref: Jacob said to Laban, “The time is up, and I want to marry Rachel now!”
text: Di saha ka Yaakubu daa yɛli Leeban, “N tum tuma maa paai di tariga, zaŋmi m paɣa maa ti ma ka n zaŋ o niŋ m paɣa.”

ref: So Laban gave a big feast and invited all their neighbors.
text: Ka Leeban daa laɣim nimaaninim’ zaa na ka bɛ ti puhi chuɣu.

ref: But that evening he brought Leah to Jacob, who married her and spent the night with her.
text: Amaa ka yuŋ daa ti paai ka o zaŋla o bipuɣiŋga Liya nti ti Yaakubu ka o zaŋ o niŋ o paɣa.

ref: Laban also gave Zilpah to Leah as her servant woman.
text: Leeban daa zaŋla o dab’ bipuɣiŋga Zilipa ti Liya ni o sɔŋd’ o.

ref: The next morning Jacob found out that he had married Leah, and he asked Laban, “Why did you do this to me? Didn't I work to get Rachel? Why did you trick me?”
text: Ka biɛɣu daa neei, ka di nyɛla Liya. Ka Yaakubu daa bɔhi Leeban, “Bɔ n-niŋ ka a niŋ ma lala? Reechɛl zuɣu ka n tum tuma ti a, ka bɔ n-niŋ ka a yɔhim ma?”

ref: Laban replied, “In our country the older daughter must get married first.
text: Ka Leeban yɛl’ o, “Di pala ti tiŋgbɔŋ ŋɔ ni kali ni bipuɣiŋga ŋun nyɛ biasani daŋ o biɛli doo kulibu.

ref: After you spend this week with Leah, you may also marry Rachel. But you will have to work for me another seven years.”
text: Chɛ ka ŋumbɔŋɔ amiliya dakulo ŋɔ naai pɔi, ka ti zaŋ ŋuni maa gba ti a, ka a tum tuma o zuɣu ti ma yuma ayopɔin pahi.”

ref: At the end of the week of celebration, Laban let Jacob marry Rachel, and he gave her his servant woman Bilhah. Jacob loved Rachel more than he did Leah, but he had to work another seven years for Laban.
text: Ka Yaakubu daa zaŋ Reechɛl niŋ o paɣa, ka yur’ o gari Liya; ka daa lan tum tuma yuma ayopɔin ti Leeban. Yaakubu ni dɔɣi bi’ shɛba

ref: The Lord knew that Jacob loved Rachel more than he did Leah, and so he gave children to Leah, but not to Rachel.
text: Yawɛ ni daa nya ka Yaakubu je Liya maa, ka o ti Liya nama, ka chɛ ka Reechɛl nyɛ paɣiŋoo.

ref: Leah gave birth to a son and named him Reuben. Then she said, “The Lord has taken away my sorrow. Now my husband will love me more than he does Rachel.”
text: Ka Liya daa niŋ pua n-dɔɣi bidibiga m-bol’ o yuli Rubin. Dama o daa yɛliya, Yawɛ nya m muɣisigu ni nyɛ shɛli; “Yɛlimaŋli, n yidan’ ni yu ma pumpɔŋɔ.”

ref: She had a second son and named him Simeon, because she said, “The Lord has heard that my husband doesn't love me.”
text: Ka o daa lan niŋ pua n-dɔɣi bidibiga ka yɛli, “Yawɛ ni baŋ ni bɛ je ma zuɣu ka o ti ma bidibiga ŋɔ gba;” ka daa bol’ o yuli Simiɔn.

ref: When Leah's third son was born, she said, “Now my husband will hold me close.” So this son was named Levi.
text: Ka o daa lan niŋ pua n-dɔɣi bidibiga ka yɛli, “Pumpɔŋɔ n yidan’ yipa ni zaŋ omaŋ’ tabili ma, dama n dɔɣi bidibis’ ata ti o;” dinzuɣu ka o daa bol’ o yuli Lɛvi.

ref: She had one more son and named him Judah, because she said, “I'll praise the Lord!”
text: Ka o daa lan niŋ pua n-dɔɣi bidibiga m-bol’ o yuli Juda ka yɛli, “Pumpɔŋɔ dina n ni paɣi Yawɛ.” Di nyaaŋa ka o daa chɛ dɔɣibu.

ref: Rachel was very jealous of Leah for having children, and she said to Jacob, “I'll die if you don't give me some children!”
text: Reechɛl ni daa nya ka o bi dɔɣi bihi ti Yaakubu maa zuɣu ka o daa zabir’ o biɛli nyuli, ka yɛli Yaakubu, “Tim’ ma bihi, di yi pa lala, n ni kpi.”

ref: But Jacob became upset with Rachel and answered, “Don't blame me! I'm not God.”
text: Ka Yaakubu daa yiɣis’ o suhu bahi Reechɛl zuɣu pam, ka bɔh’ o, “Mani n-nyɛ Naawuni ŋun ŋar’ a dɔɣim soli?”

ref: “Here, take my servant Bilhah,” Rachel told him. “Have children by her, and I'll let them be born on my knees to show that they are mine.”
text: Di saha ka Reechɛl daa yɛli Yaakubu, “N dab’ bipuɣiŋga Biliha m-bɔŋɔ; zaŋm’ o dɔni ka o dɔɣi bihi ti ma, ka n zaŋ ba ʒili n naba zuɣu.”

ref: and they had a son.
text: Ka Biliha daa niŋ pua n-dɔɣi bidibiga ti Yaakubu.

ref: Rachel named him Dan, because she said, “God has answered my prayers. He judged in my favor and has given me a son.”
text: Di saha ka Reechɛl daa yɛli, “Naawuni zahimmi n yɛla nya, ka deei n suhigu ka ti ma bidibiga”; dinzuɣu ka o daa bol’ o yuli Dan.

ref: Rachel said, “I've struggled hard with my sister, and I've won!” So she named the boy Naphtali.
text: Di saha ka Reechɛl daa yɛli, “N ni va m biɛli val’ shɛli din kpɛm’ pam la zuɣu ka n di nasara ŋɔ”; ka daa boli bia maa yuli Naftali.

ref: When Leah realized she could not have any more children, she let Jacob marry her servant Zilpah,
text: Liya ni daa nya ka o bi lan dɔɣiri maa, ka o daa zaŋ o dab’ bipuɣiŋga Zilipa ti Yaakubu ka o niŋ o paɣa.

ref: “I'm really lucky,” Leah said, and she named the boy Gad.
text: Ka Liya daa yɛli, “Zuɣusuŋ”; ka daa boli bia maa yuli Gaad.

ref: When they had another son,
text: Ka Zilipa daa lan dɔɣi bidibiga ti Yaakubu pahi.

ref: Leah exclaimed, “I'm happy now, and all the women will say how happy I am.” So she named him Asher. Love Flowers
text: Ka Liya daa yɛli, “N suhu paligiya! Dama paɣiba ni boli ma suhupiɛlli”; ka daa boli bia maa yuli Asha.

ref: During the time of the wheat harvest, Reuben found some love flowers and took them to his mother Leah. Rachel asked Leah for some of them,
text: Ka Rubin daa ti chaŋ mɔɣu ni alikama chɛbu saha ti bo ŋmaankpihiga na ti ti o ma Liya. Ka Reechɛl daa yɛli Liya, “N suhir’ a, niŋm’ a bia ŋmaankpihiga maa shɛli ti ma.”

ref: but Leah said, “It's bad enough that you stole my husband! Now you want my son's love flowers too.” “All right,” Rachel answered. “Let me have the flowers, and you can sleep with Jacob tonight.”
text: Ka Liya bɔh’ o, “A ni fa n yidan’ maa nyɛla yɛli bila? Ka a lan bɔri ni a fa n ŋmaankpihiga gba pahi?” Ka Reechɛl yɛl’ o, “Kpɛm’ o sani zuŋɔ yuŋ ŋɔ a bia ŋmaankpihiga maa zuɣu.”

ref: That evening when Jacob came in from the fields, Leah told him, “You're sleeping with me tonight. I hired you with my son's love flowers.” They slept together that night,
text: Ka Yaakubu daa ti yi mɔɣu ni na zaawuni, ka Liya yi nti tuh’ o soli yɛl’ o, “N yɛn kpɛla a sanna zuŋɔ, dama n zaŋla m bia ŋmaankpihiga yo a zuɣu.” Ka Yaakubu min’ o daa dɔni dindali maa yuŋ.

ref: and God answered Leah's prayers by giving her a fifth son.
text: Ka Naawuni daa deei Liya suhigu ka o niŋ pua n-dɔɣi bidibiga ŋun pah’ anu ti Yaakubu.

ref: Leah shouted, “God has rewarded me for letting Jacob marry my servant,” and she named the boy Issachar.
text: Ka Liya daa yɛli, “N ni zaŋ n dab’ bipuɣiŋga ti n yidan’ la zuɣu, Naawuni deei n suhigu;” ka boli bia maa yuli Isaka.

ref: she exclaimed, “God has given me a wonderful gift, and my husband will praise me for giving him six sons.” So she named the boy Zebulun.
text: Di saha ka Liya daa yɛli, “Naawuni ti ma pin’ suŋ, pumpɔŋɔ n yidan’ ni ti ma jilima, dama n dɔɣi bidibis’ ayɔbu ti o”; ka daa boli bia maa yuli Zɛbulɔn.

ref: Later, Leah had a daughter and named her Dinah.
text: Dimbɔŋɔ nyaaŋa ka o daa ti dɔɣi bipuɣiŋga m-bol’ o yuli Daina.

ref: Finally, God remembered Rachel—he answered her prayer by giving her a son. “God has taken away my disgrace,” she said.
text: Ka Reechɛl daa niŋ pua n-dɔɣi bidibiga ka yɛli, “Naawuni yihi ma vi ni;”

ref: “I'll name the boy Joseph, and I'll pray that the Lord will give me another son.” Jacob and Laban
text: ka daa boli bia maa yuli Yisifu ka yɛli, “Yawɛ tim ma bidib’ so pahi.” Yaakubu mini Leeban yɛla

ref: After Joseph was born, Jacob said to Laban, “Release me from our agreement and let me return to my own country.
text: Reechɛl ni daa dɔɣi Yisifu nyaaŋa ka Yaakubu daa yɛli Leeban, “Bahim’ ma ka n kuli n ya ni n yiŋa.

ref: You know how hard I've worked for you, so let me take my wives and children and leave.”
text: Zaŋmi m paɣiba mini m bi’ shɛb’ zuɣu n ni jɛm a ŋɔ ti ma ka n kuli, dama a mi n ni tum tuun’ shɛli ti a.”

ref: Jacob answered: You've seen how hard I've worked for you, and you know how your flocks and herds have grown under my care.
text: Ka Yaakubu yɛl’ o, “Amaŋmaŋ’ mi n ni tum shɛm ti a, ka lan mi n zuɣu ni chɛ ka a niɣi yɛligi shɛm.

ref: You didn't have much before I came, but the Lord has blessed everything I have ever done for you. Now it's time for me to start looking out for my own family.
text: N ni daa kana saha shɛl’ la, bɛ daa nyɛla biɛla; ka pumpɔŋɔ bɛ yipa nabimi pam, ka n yi kul ŋmaligi kpa luɣ’ shɛlikam polo, Yawɛ niŋdila alibarika niŋ a ni. Amaa ka pumpɔŋɔ, bɔ saha ka man’ lee yɛn zaŋ wolim gbaai ti n yiŋnim’ gba?”

ref: “How much do you want me to pay you?” Laban asked. Then Jacob told him: I don't want you to pay me anything. Just do one thing, and I'll take care of your sheep and goats.
text: Ka o bɔh’ o, “Ka ala ka n lee yɛn yo a?” Ka Yaakubu yɛl’ o, “A yi ni niŋ n ni yɛn yɛli shɛm ŋɔ, di bi kpa talahi ni a yo ma shɛli. N na kul ni gul’ a biŋkɔbiri maa.

ref: Let me go through your flocks and herds and take the sheep and goats that are either spotted or speckled and the black lambs. That's all you need to give me.
text: Chɛ ka n kpe a biŋkɔbiri maa ni zuŋɔ ti gahim piɛ’ shɛb’ bɛn liɣisiliɣisi mini bɛn mali sabilimsabilim ni piɛbihi sabila ni bu’ shɛb’ bɛn mali sabilimsabilim mini bɛn liɣisiliɣisi ni bubihi sabila; ka bɛ leei n yɔri.

ref: In the future you can easily find out if I've been honest. Just look and see if my animals are either spotted or speckled, or if the lambs are black. If they aren't, they've been stolen from you.
text: Ka m biɛrimaŋtali tuma ni ti yi palo a yi ti kana ni a ti nya n yɔri maa dahin’ shɛli. Ka bu’ shɛb’ mini piɛbihi shɛb’ bɛn bi liɣisiliɣisi ka mali sabilimsabilim ni bɛn nyɛ zaɣ’ sabila yi ti be m biŋkɔbiri ni, di wuhiya ni n zu ba mi maa.”

ref: “I agree to that,” was Laban's response.
text: Ka Leeban daa yɛli, “Lala viɛla. Ti ni niŋ a ni yɛli shɛm maa.”

ref: Before the end of the day, Laban had separated his spotted and speckled animals and the black lambs from the others and had put his sons in charge of them.
text: Amaa ka Leeban daa gahim bula’ shɛb’ bɛn mali bomaboma ni sabilimsabilim mini bunyama bɛn zaa liɣisiliɣisi ni bɛn mali sabilimsabilim ni bɛn zaa mali piɛlimpiɛlim ni piɛbihi shɛb’ bɛn zaa nyɛ zaɣ’ sabila n-zaŋ ba ti o bihi ni bɛ guli ba,

ref: Then Laban made Jacob keep the rest of the sheep and goats at a distance of three days' journey.
text: ka daa chaŋ luɣ’ shɛli katiŋa din nyɛ daba ata soli chandi ka chɛ Yaakubu, ka Yaakubu daa gbib’ ŋun’ Leeban biŋkɔb’ shɛb’ bɛn kpalim la.

ref: Jacob cut branches from some poplar trees and from some almond and evergreen trees. He peeled off part of the bark and made the branches look spotted and speckled.
text: Di saha ka Yaakubu daa chaŋ ti ŋmahi tihi balibu diba ata wula n-kɔrisikɔrisi di paɣiri ka chɛ ka di mali piɛlimpiɛlim, ka chɛ ka di piɛlimpiɛlim maa be palo ni ka di piɛlipiɛla.

ref: Then he put the branches where the sheep and goats would see them while they were drinking from the water trough. The goats mated there
text: Ka o daa zaŋ o ni kɔrisikɔrisi da’ shɛŋa maa sɔŋ biŋkɔbiri maa tooni kɔnyuri bɔɣili gbini luɣ’ shɛli biŋkɔbiri maa ni kanna ti nyuri kom. Ka biŋkɔbiri maa daa yi ti kana ni bɛ ti nyu kom ka duri taba,

ref: Some of the sheep that Jacob was keeping for Laban were already spotted. And when the others were ready to mate, he made sure that they faced in the direction of the spotted and black ones. In this way, Jacob built up a flock of sheep for himself and did not put them with the other sheep.
text: Ka Yaakubu daa woligi piɛbihi bɛ ko, ka ŋmaligi biŋkɔbiri maa nina kpa Leeban biŋkɔbiri bɛn mali bomaboma mini zaɣ’ sabila zaa la polo; ka wolig’ o biŋkɔbiri zali bɛ kɔŋko ka bi chɛ ka bɛ laɣim Leeban biŋkɔbiri maa ni.

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ka biŋkɔb’ shɛb’ bɛn mali yaa daa yi ti duri taba, Yaakubu zaŋdila dari maa sɔŋ bɛ tooni kɔbɔɣili maa gbini ni bɛ be dari maa gbini duri taba.

ref: But he would not put out the branches when the weaker animals were mating. So Jacob got all of the healthy animals, and Laban got what was left.
text: Amaa o daa bi zaŋdi dari maa sɔŋdi biŋkɔb’ shɛb’ bɛn ka yaa la bɛn’ sani, ka di zuɣu daa chɛ ka Leeban biŋkɔbiri nyɛ zaɣ’ gbarima, ka Yaakubu dina nyɛ bɛn mali yaa,

ref: Jacob soon became rich and successful. He owned many sheep, goats, camels, and donkeys, as well as a lot of slaves.
text: Ka lala zuɣu daa chɛ ka Yaakubu leei bundana ka mali biŋkɔbiri pam, ka mali daba bɛn nyɛ dɔbba mini paɣiba ni laakumnim’ ni bunsi.

ref: Jacob heard that Laban's sons were complaining, “Jacob is now a rich man, and he got everything he owns from our father.”
text: Di saha ka Yaakubu daa wum ni Leeban bidibisi yɛrimi, “Yaakubu n-fa ti ba binshɛɣukam zaa, ka binshɛlikam din nyɛ ti ba dini, dina ka o zaŋ nya buni.”

ref: Jacob also noticed that Laban was not as friendly as he had been before.
text: Ka Yaakubu daa nya ka Leeban bi lan bɔr’ o yɛla kaman o ni daa pun bɔr’ o yɛla shɛm la.

ref: One day the Lord said, “Jacob, go back to your relatives in the land of your ancestors, and I will be with you.”
text: Di saha ka Yawɛ daa yɛli Yaakubu, “Labim’ a ba min’ a dɔɣiriba tiŋgban’ ni. N ni be a sani.”

ref: Jacob sent for Rachel and Leah to meet him in the pasture where he kept his sheep,
text: Dinzuɣu ka Yaakubu daa tim ti boli Reechɛl mini Liya ni bɛ ka o sanna o ni daa mal’ o biŋkɔbiri be mɔɣu ni luɣ’ shɛli la.

ref: and he told them: Your father isn't as friendly with me as he used to be, but the God my ancestors worshiped has been on my side.
text: Ka o daa ti yɛli ba, “N nyaya ka yi ba bi lan bɔri n yɛla kaman o ni daa yi bɔri n yɛla shɛm la. Amaa m ba Naawuni la kul be n sani.

ref: years to buy your sheep and goats. During that time you kept changing my wages.
text: Yi mi ni n tum tuma ni n yaa zaa ti yi ba.

ref: and that he keeps cheating me by changing my wages time after time. But God has protected me.
text: Amaa ka o ŋari ma. O taɣi n yɔri la siɣimsiɣim bupia, amaa Naawuni bi saɣi ka o niŋ ma shɛli.

ref: When your father said the speckled sheep would be my wages, all of them were speckled. And when he said the spotted ones would be mine, all of them were spotted.
text: O yi yɛli, ‘Bu’ shɛb’ bɛn liɣisiliɣisi ka n yɛn zaŋ yo a,’ buhi maa zaa ti dɔɣirila bubihi shɛb’ bɛn liɣisiliɣisi; ka o yi lan yɛli, ‘Bu’ shɛb’ bɛn mali bomaboma ka n yɛn zaŋ yo a,’ buhi maa zaa ti dɔɣirila bubihi shɛb’ bɛn mali bomaboma.

ref: That's how God has taken sheep and goats from your father and given them to me.
text: Lala ka Naawuni niŋ zaŋ yi ba biŋkɔbiri ti ma.

ref: Once, when the flocks were mating, I dreamed that all the rams were either spotted or speckled.
text: “Biŋkɔbiri maa tab’ dubu saha ka n daa ti zahim zahindi nya ka bulahi bɛn duri buhi maa nyɛla zaɣ’ bomaboma mini bɛn liɣisiliɣisi ni bɛn nyɛ zaɣ’ bɔribɔri.

ref: Then God's angel called me by name. I answered,
text: Di saha ka Naawuni malaika daa yɛli ma zahindi maa ni, ‘Yaakubu,’ ka n saɣi yɛli, ‘N-nyɛ ma!’

ref: and he said, “Notice that all the rams are either spotted or speckled. I know everything Laban is doing to you,
text: Ka o yɛli ma, ‘Kpuɣim’ a zuɣu lihi nya bula shɛb’ zaa bɛn duri buhi ŋɔ, bɛ nyɛla zaɣ’ bomaboma mini bɛn liɣisiliɣisi ni bɛn nyɛ zaɣ’ bɔribɔri, dama n nya Leeban ni niŋd’ a shɛm.

ref: and I am the God you worshiped at Bethel, when you poured olive oil on a rock and made a promise to me. Leave here at once and return to the land where you were born.”
text: Mani n-nyɛ Bɛtɛl Naawuni, luɣ’ shɛl’ a ni daa booi kpam bahi bimbini zuɣu ka po pɔri n san’ la. Yiɣisim’ tiŋgbɔŋ ŋɔ ni lab’ bɛ ni daa dɔɣ’ a tiŋgbɔn shɛl’ ni pumpɔŋɔ’ ”

ref: He treats us like foreigners and has even cheated us out of the bride price that should have been ours.
text: O lihi ti la tinzunnima. O zaŋ ti mi kɔhi, ka mali bɛ ni zaŋ laɣ’ shɛli yo ti zuɣu maa dira.

ref: So do whatever God tells you to do. Even the property God took from our father and gave to you really belongs to us and our children.
text: Naawuni ni deei binshɛlikam zaa ti ba sani maa nyɛla ti mini ti bihi dini; dinzuɣu Naawuni ni yɛl’ a ni a niŋm’ shɛlikam zaa, nyin’ niŋm’ li.”

ref: Then Jacob, his wives, and his children got on camels and left
text: Di saha ka Yaakubu daa zaŋ o paɣiba min’ o bihi balisi laakumnima,

ref: northern Syria for the home of his father Isaac in Canaan. Jacob took along all his flocks, herds, and other property.
text: ka taɣ’ o niɣi min’ o biŋkɔbbaligu shɛb’ zaa bɛn daa nyɛ o nyɔri m-pah’ o ni daa zali naɣ’ shɛb’ Mɛsopoteemia la zuɣu n-lu soli kuni Keenan tiŋgbɔŋ ni o ba Yisahaku sani.

ref: Before Rachel left, she stole the household idols while Laban was out shearing his sheep.
text: Leeban daa chaŋla o piɛri kɔbiri chɛbu, ka Reechɛl daa zu n-kpuɣ’ o ba wuna.

ref: When Jacob crossed the Euphrates River and headed for the hill country of Gilead, he took with him everything he owned. Laban Catches Up with Jacob
text: O daa zaŋla o ni mali binshɛɣukam zaa yiɣisi zo n-chaŋ ti gbaai Yufretiiz Mɔɣili du n-zaŋ o zuɣu kpa zoya ni tin’ shɛli din yuli booni Giliad la polo. Leeban kari Yaakubu naba

ref: Three days later Laban found out that Jacob had gone.
text: Di daba ata dali ka bɛ daa ti yɛli Leeban ni Yaakubu yiɣisiya zo.

ref: So he took some of his relatives along and chased after Jacob for seven days, before catching up with him in the hill country of Gilead.
text: Ka Leeban daa zaŋ o dɔɣiriba pa omaŋ’ zuɣu n-kari Yaakubu naba daba ayopɔin hal ti mir’ o zoya ni tiŋ’ shɛli din yuli daa booni Giliad maa.

ref: But God appeared to Laban in a dream that night and warned, “Don't say a word to Jacob. Don't make a threat or a promise.”
text: Amaa ka Naawuni daa ka Leeban ŋun nyɛ Aram nir’ maa sanna yuŋ zahindi ni ti yɛl’ o, “Miri ka a yɛli Yaakubu yɛtɔɣ’ viɛlli bee yɛtɔɣ’ biɛɣu.”

ref: Jacob had set up camp in the hill country of Gilead, when Laban and his relatives came and set up camp in another part of the hill country. Laban went to Jacob
text: Ka Leeban daa kana ti paai Yaakubu. Yaakubu daa tila o suɣa zali zoya ni, ka Leeban mi min’ o dɔɣiriba maa daa ti bɛ suɣa zali zoya ni tin’ shɛli din yuli booni Giliad la.

ref: and said: Look what you've done! You've tricked me and run off with my daughters like a kidnapper.
text: Ka Leeban daa bɔhi Yaakubu, “Bɔzuɣu ka a ŋari ma zaŋ m bipuɣinsi zo kaman tɔbu ka a tuhi gbahi ba maa? Tuun’ bɔ ka a lee tum ma maa?

ref: Why did you sneak away without telling me? I would have given you a going-away party with singing and with music on tambourines and harps.
text: Bɔ n-niŋ ka a ŋari ma ka sɔɣi zo, ka bi yɛli ma. N naan chɛbis’ a ni suhupiɛlli, ka ŋme goonjɛnim’ ka chaɣisi sayɛya ka yili yila niŋ a.

ref: You didn't even give me a chance to kiss my own grandchildren and daughters goodbye. That was really foolish.
text: Bɔ n-niŋ ka a bi chɛ ka m mɔɣisi n yaansi maa mini m bipuɣinsi maa kparima n-chɛbisi ba? A tum gala.

ref: I could easily hurt you, but the God your father worshiped has warned me not to make any threats or promises.
text: M mali yiko ni n di niŋ a zaɣ’ biɛɣu, amaa a ba Yisahaku Naawuni la sa yɛli ma yuŋ la, ‘Miri ka a yɛli Yaakubu yɛtɔɣ’ viɛlli bee zaɣ’ biɛɣu.’

ref: I can understand why you were eager to return to your father, but why did you have to steal my idols?
text: A ni bɔr’ a ba yiŋ’ la zuɣu ka a yiɣisi zɔri maa, ka bɔ n-niŋ ka a zu n-kpuɣi n wuna?”

ref: Jacob answered, “I left secretly because I was afraid you would take your daughters from me by force.
text: Ka Yaakubu daa garigi yɛli Leeban, “N daa zɔr’ a mi, dama n daa tɛhimi ni di pa shɛli ka a ti zaŋ kiŋkansi fa a bipuɣinsi maa n sani.

ref: If you find that any one of us has taken your idols, I'll have that person killed. Let your relatives be witnesses. Show me what belongs to you, and you can take it back.” Jacob did not realize that Rachel had stolen the household idols.
text: Amaa a yi nya a wuna maa so sani, bɛn’ kum’ o. Lihimi n nɛma ŋɔ ni nya, a bini yi be di ni, nyin’ yihim’ li ti dɔɣiriba ŋɔ zaa ninni.” Yaakubu daa bi mi ni Reechɛl n-zu n-kpuɣi wuna maa.

ref: Laban searched the tents of Jacob, Leah, and the two servant women, but did not find the idols. Then he went to Rachel's tent.
text: Ka Leeban daa kpe Yaakubu suɣili ni n-yi nimaani kpe Liya suɣili ni, ka yi nimaani kpe dabipaɣiba ayi la suɣa ni, amaa ka bi nya shɛli di ni. Ka o daa yi Liya suɣili ni chaŋ ti kpe Reechɛl suɣili ni.

ref: She had already hidden them in the cushion she used as a saddle and was sitting on it. Laban searched everywhere and did not find them.
text: Di saha ka Reechɛl daa kpuɣi wuna maa n-zaŋ niŋ laakum gal’ ni n-du n-ʒi di zuɣu. Ka Leeban daa vihi suɣili maa ni zaa bo li ka bi nya li.

ref: Rachel said, “Father, please don't be angry with me for not getting up; I'm having my period.” Laban kept on searching, but still did not find the idols.
text: Ka Reechɛl daa yɛl’ o ba, “N dana, di je suli niŋ ma n ni ku tooi yiɣisi zani a tooni maa zuɣu, dama paɣitali biɛhigu ni ka m be.” Ka Leeban daa vihi bo wuni maa ka bi nya li.

ref: Jacob became very angry and said to Laban: What have I done wrong? Have I committed some crime? Is that why you hunted me down?
text: Di saha ka Yaakubu suhu daa yiɣisi bahi Leeban zuɣu ka o bɔhi Leeban, “Taal’ bɔ ka n lee tum’ a? Ka alahichi dini ka n lee tum ka a kariti n naba na?

ref: After searching through everything I have, did you find anything of yours? If so, put it here, where your relatives and mine can see it. Then we can decide what to do.
text: A ni vihi m binshɛɣukam zaa ni maa, a yiŋ’ bimbɔ ka a nya di ni? Zaŋm’ li zali n dɔɣiriba min’ a dɔɣiriba ŋɔ ninni, ka bɛ baŋ ti ni ŋun mali yɛlimaŋli.

ref: If a wild animal killed one of your sheep or goats, I paid for it myself. In fact, you demanded the full price, whether the animal was killed during the day or at night.
text: Mɔɣu ni biŋkɔbiri daa yi gbaag’ a piɛɣu, m bi zaŋd’ o na ti wuhir’ a, amaa n daa yi gbaarila mmaŋmaŋ’ piɛɣu zal’ o zaani. Bɛ daa yi zu binshɛli wuntaŋ’ ni bee yuŋ, a chɛrimi ka n yo a li.

ref: I sweated every day, and I couldn't sleep at night because of the cold.
text: Wuntaŋ’ daa kul wɔbiri ma mi dabisili kam, ka wari mi kpɛri ma yuŋ, ka n gaani gom.

ref: If the fearsome God worshiped by Abraham and my father Isaac had not been on my side, you would have sent me away without a thing. But God saw my hard work, and he knew the trouble I was in, so he helped me. Then last night he told you how wrong you were. Jacob and Laban Make an Agreement
text: Ka n yaannim’ Naawuni ŋun nyɛ Ibrahimma Naawuni ka nyɛ Yisahaku ni jɛmdi so ŋɔ yi di ka n sani, biɛhiŋ kani, a naan chɛ ka n chaŋ ni nuzaŋa. Naawuni nya n wahala mini n ni tum tuun’ shɛŋa, ka sa kpah’ a zuɣu ni yuŋ maa.” Yaakubu mini Leeban lo tab’ alikauli

ref: Laban said to Jacob, “Leah and Rachel are my daughters, and their children belong to me. All these sheep you are taking are really mine too. In fact, everything you have belongs to me. But there is nothing I can do to keep my daughters and their children.
text: Ka Leeban daa yɛli Yaakubu, “Bipuɣinsi ŋɔ nyɛla m bipuɣinsi, ka bihi ŋɔ nyɛ m bihi, ka biŋkɔbiri ŋɔ nyɛ m biŋkɔbiri; ka a ni nya binshɛɣukam zaa kpe ŋɔ nyɛla n dini. Amaa zuŋɔ dabisili ŋɔ wula ka n yɛn niŋ m bipuɣinsi ŋɔ mini bɛ ni dɔɣi bi’ shɛb’ ŋɔ?

ref: So I am ready to make an agreement with you, and we will pile up some large rocks here to remind us of the agreement.”
text: Pumpɔŋɔ chɛ ka m min’ a lo tab’ alikauli, ka chɛ ka di leei shɛhira m min’ a sunsuuni.”

ref: After Jacob had set up a large rock,
text: Di saha ka Yaakubu daa pii kuɣili sa nimaani ni di nyɛ din teeri ba.

ref: he told his men to get some more rocks and pile them up next to it. Then Jacob and Laban ate a meal together beside the rocks.
text: Ka Yaakubu daa yɛl’ o dɔɣiriba, “Pihimiya kuɣa na,” ka bɛ daa pihi kuɣa maa na nti kahi li duhi, ka daa ʒini kuɣ’ duli maa gbini di bindirigu.

ref: Laban named the pile of rocks Jegar Sahadutha. But Jacob named it Galeed.
text: Ka Leeban daa boli di yuli Jɛgarisahaduta; ka Yaakubu mi boli di yuli Galiid.

ref: Laban said to Jacob, “This pile of rocks will remind us of our agreement.” That's why the place was named Galeed.
text: Ka Leeban daa yɛli Yaakubu, “Kuɣ’ duli ŋɔ nyɛla shɛhira m min’ a sunsuuni zuŋɔ dabisili ŋɔ.” Dinzuɣu ka bɛ daa boli di yuli Galiid.

ref: Laban also said, “This pile of rocks means that the Lord will watch us both while we are apart from each other.” So the place was also named Mizpah.
text: Ka Leeban daa yɛli, “Ti ni yɛn woligi tab’ ŋɔ, n suhiri Yawɛ ni o gul’ ti.” Dinzuɣu ka bɛ daa lan boli nimaani yuli Mizipa.

ref: Then Laban said: If you mistreat my daughters or marry other women, I may not know about it, but remember, God is watching us!
text: Leeban daa lan yɛli, “A yi nahim m bipuɣinsi ŋɔ bee a yi kpuɣi paɣiba pahi bɛ zuɣu, ka n ʒi di yɛla, nyin’ teemi ni Naawuni n nyɛ m min’ a shɛhiralana.

ref: Both this pile of rocks and this large rock have been set up between us as a reminder. I must never go past them to attack you, and you must never come past them to attack me.
text: Kuɣ’ duli ŋɔ mini kuɣ’ shɛli din yɛn teeri ti ŋɔ nyɛla shɛhira din wuhiri ni n ku yaɣ’ kuɣ’ duli maa zuɣu ka a sanna ti niŋ a zaɣ’ biɛɣu, ka a gba ku yaɣ’ kuɣ’ duli maa zuɣu ka n sanna ti niŋ ma zaɣ’ biɛɣu.

ref: My father Nahor, your grandfather Abraham, and their ancestors all worshiped the same God, and he will make sure that we each keep the agreement. Then Jacob made a promise in the name of the fearsome God his father Isaac had worshiped.
text: Ibrahimma Naawuni mini Nahɔr Naawuni ni bɛ yaannim’ Naawuni ŋɔ ni woligi yɛlimaŋli ti ti.” Ka Yaakubu daa po o ba Yisahaku ni jɛmdi Naawuni so maa.

ref: Jacob killed an animal and offered it as a sacrifice there on the mountain, and he invited his men to eat with him. After the meal they spent the night on the mountain.
text: Ka o daa mali sara zoli zuɣu n-laɣim’ o dɔɣiriba ni bɛ kana ka bɛ min’ o ti laɣim di bindirigu, ka bɛ daa kana ka bɛ min’ o ti laɣim di bindirigu maa ka kpalim be zoli maa zuɣu yuŋ hal ka biɛɣu ti neei.

ref: Early the next morning, Laban kissed his daughters and his grandchildren goodbye, then he left to go back home.
text: Ka Leeban daa ti daŋ yiɣisibu asibaasi m-mɔɣis’ o yaansi min’ o bipuɣinsi maa kparima, ka niŋ alibarika niŋ bɛ ni ka lab’ kuli.

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Ka Yaakubu daa yiɣisi chani ka Naawuni malaikanim’ chirig’ o sol’ ni;

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: ka o daa nya ba ka yɛli, “Naawuni sansani m-bɔŋɔ!” Dinzuɣu ka o daa boli nimaani yuli Mahanaim.

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: Ka Yaakubu daa tim tuumba o biɛli Iso sani Siɛr Ɛdom tiŋgbɔŋ ni, ka bɛ daŋ o tooni.

ref: Jacob told them to say to Esau, “Master, I am your servant! I have lived with Laban all this time,
text: O daa wuhi ba: “Yi ni ti yɛn yɛli n dan’ Iso shɛm m-bɔŋɔ: ‘A dabili Yaakubu ni yɛli shɛm m-bɔŋɔ: “N daa chaŋ ti bela Leeban sani hal ti paai pumpɔŋɔ.

ref: and now I own cattle, donkeys, and sheep, as well as many slaves. Master, I am sending these messengers in the hope that you will be kind to me.”
text: M mali niɣi mini bunsi ni biŋkɔbbaligu ni daba bɛn nyɛ dɔbba mini paɣiba; ka tim na ni n ti yɛli nyini n dana din ni chɛ ka a suhu maai ni mani.’ ”

ref: When the messengers returned, they told Jacob, “We went to your brother Esau, and now he is heading this way with
text: Ka tuumba maa daa ti labina ti yɛli Yaakubu, “Ti chaŋ a biɛli Iso sani, ka o zaŋ ninvuɣ’ kɔbisinahi pa omaŋ’ zuɣu n-kanna ni o ti tuh’ a soli.”

ref: Jacob was so frightened that he divided his people, sheep, cattle, and camels into two groups.
text: Ka dabiɛm daa gbaai Yaakubu pam ka o suhu zɔhira. Ka o daa pirig’ o niriba bɛn be o sani min’ o biŋkɔbbaligu ni o niɣi ni o laakumnim’ ʒii buyi,

ref: He thought, “If Esau attacks one group, perhaps the other can escape.”
text: ka yɛl’ o suhu ni, “Iso yi kana ti ku ninvuɣ’ shɛb’ bɛn be tooni ŋɔ, bɛn doli nyaaŋa la ni zo n-tiligi.”

ref: Then Jacob prayed: You, Lord, are the God who was worshiped by my grandfather Abraham and by my father Isaac. You told me to return home to my family, and you promised to be with me and make me successful.
text: Di saha ka Yaakubu daa yɛli, “N yab’ Ibrahimma mini m ba Yisahaku Naawuni ŋɔ, Nyin’ Yawɛ, a daa yɛli ma, ‘Labim’ a ya min’ a dɔɣiriba sani ka n niŋ a zaɣ’ viɛlli.’

ref: Please rescue me from my brother. I am afraid he will come and attack not only me, but my wives and children as well.
text: N suhir’ a, fam’ ma m biɛli Iso nuu ni bahi. N zɔr’ o, dama di pa shɛli ka o ti kana ti ku m mini m bihi ni bɛ manim’ ŋɔ zaa.

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Amaa ka a mi daa pun yɛli ma, ‘N ni niŋ a zaɣ’ viɛlli, ka chɛ ka a zuliya ti be kaman mɔɣ’ bihigu la, ka so ku tooi kali ba.’ ”

ref: After Jacob had spent the night there, he chose some animals as gifts for Esau:
text: Ka Yaakubu daa gbe nimaani dindali maa yuŋ, ka daa piig’ o biŋkɔbiri maa ni shɛb’ ni o ti o biɛli Iso.

ref: And Jacob told them to be sure to say that he was right behind them. Jacob hoped the gifts would make Esau friendly, so Esau would be glad to see him when they met.
text: ka lan yɛl’ o, ‘Ka din pahi nyɛla, a dabili Yaakubu doli ti nyaaŋa na.’ ” Dama Yaakubu daa yɛlila o suhu ni, “N yi chɛ ka bɛ zaŋ pini ŋɔ daŋ n tooni ti ti o, n ni maag’ o suhu, ka di nyaaŋa m min’ o nini yi ti nya taba, di pa shɛli o ni deei ma.”

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Ka Yaakubu daa yɛli, “N nini nya Naawuni ka bi kpi.” Dinzuɣu ka o daa boli nimaani yuli Pɛniɛl.

ref: Jacob put servants in charge of each herd and told them, “Go ahead of me and keep a space between each herd.”

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.

ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”

ref: Jacob also told the men in charge of the second and third herds and those who followed to say the same thing when they met Esau.

ref: Jacob's men took the gifts on ahead of him, but he spent the night in camp. Jacob's Name Is Changed to Israel
text: Ka bɛ daa zaŋ pini maa daŋ o tooni, ka ŋun’ maŋmaŋ’ daa kpalim gbe suɣa maa shee dindali maa yuŋ. Yaakubu mini ninvuɣ’ so vaya Pɛnuɛl

ref: Afterwards, Jacob went back and spent the rest of the night alone. A man came and fought with Jacob until just before daybreak.
text: ka ŋun’ Yaakubu kɔŋko daa kpalim be nyaaŋa. Di saha ka do’ so daa kana ka o min’ o ti kpe val’ ni n-va hal ka biɛɣ’ ti neerina.

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Doo maa ni daa nya ka o bi tooi nyaŋ Yaakubu maa, ka o daa shih’ o sakpuɣu, ka o sakpuɣu maa ŋmirigi bɛ ni daa vari saha shɛli maa.

ref: They kept on wrestling until the man said, “Let go of me! It's almost daylight.” “You can't go until you bless me,” Jacob replied.

ref: The sun was coming up as Jacob was leaving Peniel. He was limping because he had been struck on the hip,

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Amaa ka a mi daa pun yɛli ma, ‘N ni niŋ a zaɣ’ viɛlli, ka chɛ ka a zuliya ti be kaman mɔɣ’ bihigu la, ka so ku tooi kali ba.’ ”

ref: After Jacob had spent the night there, he chose some animals as gifts for Esau:

ref: And Jacob told them to be sure to say that he was right behind them. Jacob hoped the gifts would make Esau friendly, so Esau would be glad to see him when they met.
text: ka lan yɛl’ o, ‘Ka din pahi nyɛla, a dabili Yaakubu doli ti nyaaŋa na.’ ” Dama Yaakubu daa yɛlila o suhu ni, “N yi chɛ ka bɛ zaŋ pini ŋɔ daŋ n tooni ti ti o, n ni maag’ o suhu, ka di nyaaŋa m min’ o nini yi ti nya taba, di pa shɛli o ni deei ma.”

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.

ref: Jacob put servants in charge of each herd and told them, “Go ahead of me and keep a space between each herd.”

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.
text: O daa yɛl’ o tumtumdiba kpɛma, “M biɛli Iso yi ti tuhi ya soli bɔhi ya, ‘Ŋuni n-lee sun ya? Ka ya polo ka yi lee chana? Ka ŋuni n-lee su biŋkɔb’ shɛb’ bɛn be yi tooni ŋɔ?’

ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”
text: Di saha yin’ yɛlimiy’ o, ‘A dabili Yaakubu n-su ba, ka zaŋ ba ti ti ni ti zaŋ ba na ti ti nyini n-dan’ Iso pini, ka din pahi nyɛla, omaŋmaŋ’ doli ti nyaaŋa na.’ ”

ref: Jacob also told the men in charge of the second and third herds and those who followed to say the same thing when they met Esau.
text: Lala ka o daa yɛl’ o tumtumda so ŋun pah’ ayi mini ŋun pah’ ata ni bɛn zaa daa taɣiri biŋkɔbiri maa; “Yi yi ti tuhi Iso soli, yin’ yɛlimiy’ o yɛtɔɣ’ yini maa,

ref: Jacob's men took the gifts on ahead of him, but he spent the night in camp. Jacob's Name Is Changed to Israel
text: Ka bɛ daa zaŋ pini maa daŋ o tooni, ka ŋun’ maŋmaŋ’ daa kpalim gbe suɣa maa shee dindali maa yuŋ. Yaakubu mini ninvuɣ’ so vaya Pɛnuɛl

ref: Afterwards, Jacob went back and spent the rest of the night alone. A man came and fought with Jacob until just before daybreak.
text: ka ŋun’ Yaakubu kɔŋko daa kpalim be nyaaŋa. Di saha ka do’ so daa kana ka o min’ o ti kpe val’ ni n-va hal ka biɛɣ’ ti neerina.

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Doo maa ni daa nya ka o bi tooi nyaŋ Yaakubu maa, ka o daa shih’ o sakpuɣu, ka o sakpuɣu maa ŋmirigi bɛ ni daa vari saha shɛli maa.

ref: They kept on wrestling until the man said, “Let go of me! It's almost daylight.” “You can't go until you bless me,” Jacob replied.
text: Ka doo maa daa yɛl’ o, “Biɛɣu neera, chɛ ka n chaŋ.” Ka Yaakubu yɛl’ o, “N ku chɛ ka a chaŋ, faashee ka a niŋ alibarika niŋ n ni.”

ref: Then the man asked, “What is your name?” “Jacob,” he answered.
text: Ka doo maa bɔh’ o, “A yuli?” Ka o yɛl’ o, “N yuli Yaakubu.”

ref: The man said, “From now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.”
text: Di saha ka doo maa daa yɛl’ o, “A yuli ku lan booni Yaakubu, amaa a yuli ni boonila Izraɛl, dama a mini Naawuni ni ninsalinim’ vaya ka a di nasara.”

ref: The sun was coming up as Jacob was leaving Peniel. He was limping because he had been struck on the hip,

ref: and the muscle on his hip joint had been injured. That's why even today the people of Israel don't eat the hip muscle of any animal.
text: Dinzuɣu dindali zaŋ na hal ni zuŋɔ, Izraɛlnim’ bi saɣiri ŋubiri binsakpuɣu zuɣu nimdi, doo maa ni daa shihi Yaakubu sakpuɣu maa zuɣu.

ref: The two servant women, Zilpah and Bilhah, together with their children went first, followed by Leah and her children, then by Rachel and Joseph.
text: O daa zaŋla dabipaɣiba ayi maa mini bɛ bihi bahi tooni, ka chɛ ka Liya min’ o bihi paya, ka chɛ ka Reechɛl mini Yisifu be nyaaŋa.

ref: Jacob himself walked in front of them all, bowing to the ground seven times as he came near his brother.

ref: But Esau ran toward Jacob and hugged and kissed him. Then the two brothers started crying.
text: Amaa ka Iso daa guuina ti tuh’ o soli m-bɔb’ gbaag’ o n-zaŋ o zuɣu ti pa o nyingol’ zuɣu m-mɔɣis’ o kpariŋ, ka bɛ zaa dii kuhira.

ref: When Esau noticed the women and children he asked, “Whose children are these?” Jacob answered, “These are the children the Lord has been kind enough to give to me, your servant.”
text: Ka Iso daa nya paɣiba mini bihi maa ka bɔhi Yaakubu, “Ka banima n-lee be a sani ŋɔ?” Ka Yaakubu yɛl’ o, “Naawuni ni niŋ mani a dabili ŋɔ nirilim ti ma bi’ shɛba m-bala.”

ref: Esau asked Jacob, “Why did you send those herds I met along the road?” “Master,” Jacob answered, “I sent them so you would be friendly to me.”
text: Ka Iso daa bɔhi Yaakubu, “Ka n ni nya binshɛŋa ŋɔ zaa daliri lee nyɛla dini?” Ka Yaakubu yɛl’ o, “N zaŋ ba mi na ni n ti ti a m-maai nyini n dan’ suhu.”

ref: “But, brother, I already have plenty,” Esau replied. “Keep them for yourself.”
text: Amaa ka Iso yɛl’ o, “M mabia, mal’ a bini; n gba bi kɔŋ.”

ref: “No!” Jacob said. “Please accept them as a sign of your friendship for me. When you welcomed me and I saw your face, it was like seeing the face of God.
text: Ka Yaakubu daa yɛl’ o, “Aai, n suhir’ a, a suhu yi shiri maai ni mani, nyin’ deemi m pini maa, dama n ni nya a ka a deei ma ni suhumahili maa, di ŋmanila n nini nyala Naawuni.

ref: Please accept these as gifts I brought to you. God has been good to me, and I have everything I need.” Jacob kept insisting until Esau agreed.

ref: “Let's get ready to travel,” Esau said. “I'll go along with you.”

ref: But Jacob answered, “Master, you know traveling is hard on children, and I have to look after the sheep and goats that are nursing their young. If my animals travel too much in one day, they will all die.
text: Amaa ka Yaakubu yɛl’ o, “N dana, a mi ni bihi ŋɔ ka yaa, ka biŋkɔb’ bihi bɛn mɔɣiri be biŋkɔbiri maa ni, ka di simdi ni n chɛ ka n zaɣa be bɛ ni viɛnyɛliŋga. Ti yi taɣi ba chaŋ pam dahin yini, bɛ zaa ni kpi.

ref: Why don't you go on ahead and let me travel along slowly with the children, the herds, and the flocks. We can meet again in the country of Edom.”

ref: Esau replied, “Let me leave some of my men with you.” “You don't have to do that,” Jacob answered. “I am happy, simply knowing that you are friendly to me.”

ref: But Jacob went to Succoth, where he built a house for himself and set up shelters for his animals. That's why the place is called Succoth. Jacob Arrives at Shechem

ref: The two men met with the other leaders of their city and told them:

ref: Three days later the men who had been circumcised were still weak from pain. So Simeon and Levi, two of Dinah's brothers, attacked with their swords and killed every man in the town,

ref: including Hamor and Shechem. Then they took Dinah and left.

text: ni bɛ daazichi zaa ni bɛ paɣiba mini bɛ bihi ni binshɛɣukam din daa be bɛ yinsi zaa chaŋ.

ref: They answered, “Was it right to let our own sister be treated that way?”
text: Amaa ka bɛ yɛl’ o, “Bɔzuɣu ka bɛ niŋ ti tizɔpaɣ’ maa kaman pagɔrili n-nyɛ o maa?”

ref: Afterwards, we'll go to Bethel. I will build an altar there for God, who answered my prayers when I was in trouble and who has always been at my side.
text: ka ti yiɣisi chaŋ Bɛtɛl. M bɔri ni m mɛla bimbini nimaani zali Naawuni so ŋun daa deei n suhigu n nandahima nyabu dali, ka be n sani n ni chaŋ luɣ’ shɛlikam la.”

ref: While Jacob and his family were traveling through Canaan, God terrified the people in the towns so much that no one dared bother them.

ref: While they were there, Rebekah's personal servant Deborah died. They buried her under an oak tree and called it “Weeping Oak.” God Blesses Jacob at Bethel
text: Ka Dɛbɔra ŋun daa nyɛ Ribɛka yiŋ’ dabipaɣ’ la daa kpi, ka bɛ sɔɣ’ o oki ti’ shɛli din daa be Bɛtɛl tiŋli la gbini. Ka bɛ daa boli di yuli “Kumsi tia.”

ref: After Jacob came back to the land of Canaan, God appeared to him again. This time he gave Jacob a new name and blessed him by saying: I am God All-Powerful, and from now on your name will be Israel instead of Jacob. You will have many children. Your descendants will become nations, and some of the men in your family will even be kings.
text: Ka Naawuni daa lan yɛl’ o, “Mani n-nyɛ Naawuni Kpiɔŋlan’ Naa, dɔɣim’ bihi ka yi nabi; ka zuliya pam yi a ni na; ka nanim’ pam gba ti yi a zuliya ni na.

ref: I will give you the land that I promised Abraham and Isaac, and it will belong to your family forever.
text: N ni daa zaŋ tiŋgbɔn shɛli ti Ibrahimma mini Yisahaku la, n ni zaŋ li ti a, ka ni lan zaŋ li ti a zuliya a kalinsi nyaaŋa.”

ref: Jacob set up a large rock, so that he would remember what had happened there. Then he poured wine and olive oil on the rock to show that it was dedicated to God,
text: Ka Yaakubu daa pihi kuɣa m-me bimbini zali Naawuni ni daa tɔɣis’ o yɛtɔɣa luɣ’ shɛli maa, ka booi wain mini kpam bahi di zuɣu ti Naawuni.

ref: and he named the place Bethel. Benjamin Is Born
text: Dinzuɣu ka Yaakubu daa boli Naawuni ni tɔɣis’ o yɛtɔɣa luɣ’ shɛli maa yuli Bɛtɛl. Reechɛl kum yɛla

ref: Jacob and his family had left Bethel and were still a long way from Ephrath, when the time came for Rachel's baby to be born.
text: Di nyaaŋa ka bɛ daa yi Bɛtɛl chani ti miri Ɛfirat ka Reechɛl piligi wolisibu ka o wolisibu maa daa kpɛm’ pam.

ref: She was having a rough time, but the woman who was helping her said, “Don't worry! It's a boy.”
text: Ka di ni daa ti miig’ o pam saha shɛli ka paɣidɔɣisɔtɔɣinda yɛl’ o, “Di chɛ ka a suhu zɔhira, bidibiga n-lan bala.”

ref: Rachel was at the point of death, and right before dying, she said, “I'll name him Benoni.” But Jacob called him Benjamin.
text: Amaa ka o daa lee kpalim dɔɣibu maa ni. O ni daa ti yɛn bahi saha shɛli ka o daa boli bia maa yuli Bɛnɔni, amaa ka o ba mi daa bol’ o yuli Bɛnjamin.

ref: Rachel was buried beside the road to Ephrath, which is also called Bethlehem.
text: Reechɛl ni daa kpi maa, bɛ daa sɔɣ’ o la so’ shɛli din daa kuni Ɛfirat la sokpaŋa, nimaani ka bɛ booni Bɛtilihɛm pumpɔŋɔ ŋɔ.

ref: Some years later Judah's wife died, and he mourned for her. He then went with his friend Hirah to the town of Timnah, where his sheep were being sheared.
